275	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — L3 base case `H¹(U, F) = 0` -/
276	
277	/-- **Sheaf-cohomology base case `H¹(U, F) = 0` (Stacks 01EO L3).** Given a short exact
278	sequence `S : 0 → F → I → Q → 0` in `X.Modules` with `I = S.X₂` injective, and assuming the
279	section map `I(U) → Q(U)` is surjective, the first absolute cohomology `H¹(U, F) = Ext¹(jShriekOU
280	U, F)` vanishes. The proof runs the covariant Ext long exact sequence: `H¹(U, I) = 0` by injective
281	vanishing, and surjectivity of the section map transfers (via the natural `H⁰ ≅ Γ`) to
282	surjectivity of `H⁰(U, I) → H⁰(U, Q)`, killing the connecting map. Project-local: assembles the
283	`AbsoluteCohomology` Ext wrappers into the 01EO base case. -/
284	theorem absoluteCohomology_one_eq_zero_of_basis (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
285	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) [Injective S.X₂]
286	    (hsurj : Function.Surjective
287	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map S.g).app (Opposite.op U))))
288	    (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₁ 1) : e = 0 := by
289	  have hef : e.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 1) = 0 :=
290	    absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective 0 U S.X₂ _
291	  obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ := absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₁ U hS e hef (rfl : 0 + 1 = 1)
292	  have hgsurj : Function.Surjective
293	      (fun y : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₂ 0 => y.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero 0)) := by
294	    intro z
295	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hsurj (absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U S.X₃ z)
296	    refine ⟨(absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U S.X₂).symm i, ?_⟩
297	    apply (absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U S.X₃).injective
298	    rw [absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv_naturality, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply, hi]
299	  obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ := hgsurj x₃
300	  rw [← hx₃, ← hx₂,
301	    Ext.comp_assoc x₂ (Ext.mk₀ S.g) hS.extClass (add_zero 0) (rfl : 0 + 1 = 1) rfl,
302	    hS.comp_extClass, Ext.comp_zero]
303	
304	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — cover system on a basis (01EO conditions 1–2) -/
305	
306	/-- A **covering datum** on `X`: an index family of opens (the covered open is `⨆ i, c.2 i`).
307	Project-local: the lightweight cover representation matching the cover-local Čech lemmas
308	(`cechCohomology`, `faceShortComplex`), which are indexed by a family `ι → Opens X`. -/
309	abbrev CovDatum (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type (u + 1) :=
310	  Σ ι : Type u, ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X
311	
312	/-- **Cover system on a basis** (Stacks 01EO conditions (1)–(2)). Records a basis `B`, a set of
313	admissible coverings `Cov`, the faces-in-basis datum (every finite intersection of a covering's
314	opens lies in `B`, condition (1)), and the cofinality datum in the form it is consumed —
315	condition (2) packaged as the section-surjectivity it produces through `ses_cech_h1`: for every
316	short exact sequence `S` of `O_X`-modules whose left term has vanishing higher Čech cohomology,
317	the section map of `S.g` is surjective over every basic open. The auxiliary `injective_acyclic`
318	field records the standard-cover injective Čech-acyclicity (Stacks
319	`lemma-injective-trivial-cech`, i.e. `injective_cech_acyclic`) for the system's coverings.
320	Project-local: bespoke encoding; the two sheaf-theoretic fields are discharged at the affine
321	(02KG) instantiation by `ses_cech_h1` + standard-cover cofinality and by `injective_cech_acyclic`
322	respectively. -/
323	structure BasisCovSystem (X : Scheme.{u}) where
324	  /-- The basis of opens. -/
325	  B : Set (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
326	  /-- The set of admissible coverings. -/
327	  Cov : Set (CovDatum X)
328	  /-- Condition (1): every finite intersection of a covering's opens lies in the basis. -/
329	  faces_mem : ∀ c ∈ Cov, ∀ (p : ℕ) (σ : Fin (p + 1) → c.1), (⨅ k, c.2 (σ k)) ∈ B
330	  /-- Condition (2), in the section-surjectivity shape produced by `ses_cech_h1` + cofinality. -/
331	  surj_of_vanishing : ∀ (S : ShortComplex X.Modules), S.ShortExact →
332	    (∀ c ∈ Cov, ∀ q, 0 < q →
333	      IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj S.X₁) q)) →
334	    ∀ V ∈ B, Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
335	      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map
336	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map S.g)).app (Opposite.op V)))
337	  /-- Injective Čech-acyclicity for the system's coverings (`injective_cech_acyclic`). -/
338	  injective_acyclic : ∀ (I : X.Modules), Injective I → ∀ c ∈ Cov, ∀ q, 0 < q →
339	    IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I) q)
340	
341	/-- **Vanishing higher Čech cohomology for a cover system** (Stacks 01EO condition (3)). An
342	`O_X`-module `F` satisfies this when `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, F) = 0` for every covering `𝒰 ∈ s.Cov` and every
343	`p > 0`. Stated for an arbitrary module (not necessarily quasi-coherent): the dimension-shift
344	induction feeds the quotient `Q = I/F` back as coefficient, and `Q` need not be quasi-coherent.
345	Project-local: the inductive predicate of the 01EO argument. -/
346	@[reducible] def HasVanishingHigherCech (s : BasisCovSystem X) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
347	  ∀ c ∈ s.Cov, ∀ p, 0 < p →
348	    IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) p)
349	
350	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — L4 dimension-shift induction -/
351	
352	-- The dimension-shift induction embeds modules into injectives, which needs
353	-- `EnoughInjectives X.Modules`. That instance is NOT available in Mathlib for sheaves of modules
354	-- (it would follow from `IsGrothendieckAbelian (SheafOfModules R)`, which is absent — see
355	-- `analogies/p5a.md`), so — following the project convention for the P5a derived-functor lane —
356	-- it is carried as an explicit hypothesis on `injSES`/L4 rather than synthesized.
357	
358	/-- The injective-embedding short exact sequence `0 → F → I → I/F → 0` of an `O_X`-module `F`,
359	with `I = Injective.under F` the chosen injective and `I/F` its cokernel. Project-local helper for
360	the 01EO dimension-shift induction; carries `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` as a hypothesis. -/
361	@[reducible] private noncomputable def injSES [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (F : X.Modules) :
362	    ShortComplex X.Modules :=
363	  ShortComplex.mk (Injective.ι F) (cokernel.π (Injective.ι F)) (cokernel.condition _)
364	
365	private lemma injSES_shortExact [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (F : X.Modules) :
366	    (injSES F).ShortExact :=
367	  ShortComplex.ShortExact.mk (ShortComplex.exact_cokernel _)
368	
369	/-- **Dimension-shift induction `Hᵖ(U, F) = 0` for `p > 0` (Stacks 01EO L4).** For a cover system
370	`s` on a basis and any `O_X`-module `F` with vanishing higher Čech cohomology for `s`, the
371	absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(U, F) = Extᵖ(jShriekOU U, F)` vanishes for every basic open `U ∈ s.B` and
372	`p > 0`. Induction on `p`, quantified over all `F` in the inductive class: the quotient
373	`Q = I/F` re-enters the class (via the section Čech SES of L1 from the per-face SES, plus L2
374	quotient-vanishing and the cover system's injective acyclicity), the base case is L3, and the
375	inductive step is the covariant Ext long exact sequence. Project-local: the 01EO inductive core. -/
376	theorem absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (s : BasisCovSystem X)
377	    {F : X.Modules} (hF : HasVanishingHigherCech s F)
378	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X} (hU : U ∈ s.B) {p : ℕ} (hp : 0 < p)
379	    (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F p) : e = 0 := by
380	  have key : ∀ (n : ℕ) (F : X.Modules), HasVanishingHigherCech s F →
381	      ∀ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X), U ∈ s.B →
382	        ∀ e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F (n + 1), e = 0 := by
383	    intro n
384	    induction n with
385	    | zero =>
386	      intro F hF U hU e
387	      have hS := injSES_shortExact F
388	      haveI : Injective (injSES F).X₂ := inferInstance
389	      exact absoluteCohomology_one_eq_zero_of_basis U hS
390	        (s.surj_of_vanishing (injSES F) hS hF U hU) e
391	    | succ m ih =>
392	      intro F hF U hU e
393	      have hS := injSES_shortExact F
394	      haveI : Injective (injSES F).X₂ := inferInstance
395	      -- The quotient `Q = (injSES F).X₃` again has vanishing higher Čech cohomology.
396	      have hQ : HasVanishingHigherCech s (injSES F).X₃ := by
397	        intro c hc q hq
398	        set P := (injSES F).map (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X) with hP
399	        have hface : ∀ (pp : ℕ) (σ : Fin (pp + 1) → c.1),
400	            (faceShortComplex c.2 P σ).ShortExact := by
401	          intro pp σ
402	          refine faceShortComplex_shortExact_of_sheaf_ses c.2 (injSES F) hS ?_ pp σ
403	          intro pp' σ'
404	          exact s.surj_of_vanishing (injSES F) hS hF _ (s.faces_mem c hc pp' σ')
405	        have hSES := cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis c.2 P hface
406	        exact quotient_cech_vanishing_of_basis c.2 P hSES
407	          (s.injective_acyclic (injSES F).X₂ inferInstance c hc) (hF c hc) q hq
408	      -- Ext long exact sequence step: `H^{m+2}(F)` is squeezed by `H^{m+1}(Q) = 0` (IH) and
409	      -- `H^{m+2}(I) = 0` (injective vanishing).
410	      have hef : e.comp (Ext.mk₀ (injSES F).f) (add_zero (m + 1 + 1)) = 0 :=
411	        absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective (m + 1) U (injSES F).X₂ _
412	      obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
413	        absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₁ U hS e hef (rfl : (m + 1) + 1 = m + 1 + 1)
414	      have hx₃zero : x₃ = 0 := ih (injSES F).X₃ hQ U hU x₃
415	      rw [← hx₃, hx₃zero, Ext.zero_comp]
416	  obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hp.ne'
417	  exact key n F hF U hU e
418	
419	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis (01EO top) -/
420	
421	/-- **Čech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis (Stacks 01EO).** Let `s` be a cover system on a
422	basis for `X` and `F` an `O_X`-module with vanishing higher Čech cohomology for `s` (conditions
423	(1)–(3) of 01EO, packaged in `s` and `hF`). Then the absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(U, F) =
424	Extᵖ(jShriekOU U, F)` vanishes for every basic open `U ∈ s.B` and every `p > 0`. The thin assembly
425	of the dimension-shift induction `absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis` (L4), which itself chains
426	the section-Čech short exact sequence (L1), quotient vanishing (L2), and the base case (L3).
427	Project-local: the named 01EO target, carrying `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` as a hypothesis (the
428	instance is absent in Mathlib for sheaves of modules; see `injSES`). -/
429	theorem cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (s : BasisCovSystem X)
430	    {F : X.Modules} (hF : HasVanishingHigherCech s F) :
431	    ∀ U ∈ s.B, ∀ p, 0 < p → ∀ e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F p, e = 0 :=
432	  fun _ hU _ hp e => absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis s hF hU hp e
433

495	`[IsProper C.hom]` are class arguments propagated via instance synthesis from
496	the use site (where `C` is concretely a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme, e.g.\ a
497	smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve). -/
498	noncomputable instance instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf
499	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
500	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
501	    IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) where
502	  module_finite_hom := by
503	    haveI := Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper k C
504	    let LE1 := constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv k
505	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of k k)
506	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
507	    let LE2 := homFromOne_linearEquiv k
508	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
509	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
510	    exact Module.Finite.equiv (LE1.trans LE2).symm
511	
512	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

516	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the Čech cochain complex of
517	the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`,
518	with respect to an arbitrary indexed family of opens `𝒰 : ι → Opens C.left.toTopCat`.
519	Built from Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (file
520	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
521	underlying presheaf of `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (iter-006). The result is a
522	cochain complex valued in `ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`.
523	
524	The cohomology of this complex is `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` below. The
525	downstream comparison theorem (Čech cohomology = derived-functor cohomology
526	= `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` for an acyclic cover) is
527	queued for iter-013+; iter-012 only establishes the Čech-side carrier. -/
528	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain_OC
529	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
530	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
531	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
532	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
533	
534	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the `n`-th Čech cohomology
535	of the structure sheaf for an arbitrary indexed open cover. Defined as the
536	`n`-th homology of the Čech cochain complex `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`. The
537	result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k` and therefore carries a `Module k`
538	structure for free; the iter-013+ comparison theorem will identify it
539	with `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n` when the cover is
540	acyclic. -/
541	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC
542	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
543	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
544	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
545	  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰).homology n
546	
547	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cochain complex generalising iter-012's
548	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC` to any sheaf of `k`-modules `F`, not just the structure
549	sheaf. Built from the same Mathlib `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor`
550	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
551	underlying presheaf of `F`. The result is a cochain complex valued in
552	`ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`. Iter-012's specialisation
553	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰` is recovered by setting `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`
554	(see `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` below).
555	
556	This generalisation is the foundational scaffolding the iter-048+ Čech-vs-derived
557	comparison theorem will build on: the comparison map
558	`Scheme.cechCohomology k C F 𝒰 n →ₗ[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ᵢ 𝒰 i)`
559	(queued for iter-048) is naturally parameterised over the sheaf `F`, not just
560	the structure sheaf, so the parameterised carrier is the right level of
561	generality. -/
562	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain
563	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
564	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
565	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
566	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
567	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)
568	
569	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cohomology generalising iter-012's
570	`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`. The `n`-th cohomology of the parameterised Čech
571	cochain complex. The result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k`. Iter-012's
572	specialisation is recovered by `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` below. -/
573	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology
574	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
575	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
576	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
577	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
578	  (Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰).homology n
579	
580	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCochain_OC` is definitionally the
581	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCochain`. The
582	proof is `rfl` since iter-012's body is the same `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj
583	((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))` term. Used by downstream
584	consumers (iter-048+) to switch between the iter-012 structure-sheaf-specific
585	form and the iter-047 parameterised form without semantic loss. -/
586	theorem Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq
587	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
588	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
589	    Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰 = Scheme.cechCochain C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 :=
590	  rfl
591	
592	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` is definitionally the
593	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCohomology`. -/
594	theorem Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq
595	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
596	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
597	    Scheme.cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n =
598	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n :=
599	  rfl
600	
601	/-- Iter-048: Čech-side acyclicity carrier predicate. The cover `𝒰` is
602	*Čech-acyclic for the sheaf `F` of `k`-modules* on `C` if positive-degree Čech
603	cohomology vanishes (in the `Subsingleton` sense — `Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n`
604	has type `ModuleCat.{u} k`, but `Subsingleton` on the underlying type is the
605	natural and more chainable form). Mirrors the iter-040 / iter-043 carrier-
606	predicate pattern: a single-field `Prop` class capturing a combinatorial
607	vanishing condition that downstream consumers receive as an instance argument.
608	
609	This is the foundational Čech-side input that the iter-051 producer instance for
610	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` will consume, in conjunction
611	with the iter-049+ Čech-vs-derived comparison theorem and iter-048's consumer. -/
612	class Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover
613	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
614	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
615	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) : Prop where
616	  subsingleton_cechCohomology :
617	    ∀ (n : ℕ), 0 < n → Subsingleton (Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n)
618	
619	/-- Iter-048: subsingleton transport via Čech acyclicity + comparison.
620	
621	Given the iter-048 carrier hypothesis `[IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]` AND an explicit
622	comparison iso `compIso n : cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i)`
623	(the Čech-vs-derived comparison, queued for iter-049+ to construct as a
624	theorem), conclude that `Subsingleton (HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i))` for `n ≥ 1`.
625	
626	The `compIso` is taken as an *explicit argument*, not a class field. The
627	comparison itself is a `LinearEquiv` (data), so it cannot be a field of a
628	`Prop`-valued class; more importantly, decoupling the Čech-side combinatorial
629	vanishing (iter-048) from the substantive comparison theorem (iter-049+) lets
630	each step land as a single iteration. Iter-049+ will provide the comparison as
631	a theorem, which iter-048's consumer accepts directly via this argument.
632	
633	The consumer body extracts the class field (the `Subsingleton` on
634	`cechCohomology n`) and transports along `(compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton`. -/
635	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover
636	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
637	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
638	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
639	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]
640	    (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ),
641	      Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k]
642	        Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
643	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
644	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) := by
645	  haveI := Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover.subsingleton_cechCohomology
646	    (F := F) (𝒰 := 𝒰) n hn
647	  exact (compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton
648	
649	/-- Iter-048: curve specialisation at `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.
650	
651	Mirrors the iter-039 / iter-042 / iter-043 `_curve` pattern: a thin dot-notation
652	wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is the structure
653	sheaf) from re-typing `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-048 consumer
654	is chained through. Used by the iter-051 `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
655	producer instance. -/
656	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover_curve
657	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
658	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
659	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰]
660	    (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ),
661	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k]
662	        Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
663	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
664	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) :=
665	  Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover (𝒰 := 𝒰) compIso n hn
666	
667	end AlgebraicGeometry

262	/-- **Additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The free–Yoneda hom bijection
263	`Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is an isomorphism of abelian groups: the source is
264	the (preadditive) hom-group of presheaves of modules and the target is the section group
265	`F(V)`. This is the per-multi-index additive core of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
266	(blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`); taking products over multi-indices and
267	intertwining with the differential upgrades it to the full complex isomorphism. Additivity
268	holds because, by `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, the bijection is evaluation of the
269	component `ψ.app (op V)` (a module map, hence additive) on a fixed generator, and the
270	component of a sum of morphisms is the sum of components.
271	
272	Project-local because Mathlib packages the underlying bijection (`freeHomEquiv` ∘
273	`yonedaEquiv`) only at the level of types, not as an `AddEquiv`. -/
274	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
275	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
276	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃+
277	      (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) where
278	  toFun := freeYonedaHomEquiv V F
279	  invFun := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).symm
280	  left_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).left_inv
281	  right_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).right_inv
282	  map_add' f g := by
283	    rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply,
284	      PresheafOfModules.add_app]
285	    change (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f.app (Opposite.op V) + g.app (Opposite.op V))) _ = _
286	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add]; rfl

290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
307	    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    -- The map at `𝟙 n` projects to index `σ ∘ id = σ` and restricts along the thin
311	    -- (reflexive) `Opens` hom `homOfLE ⋯ : ⨅ U(σ k) ⟶ ⨅ U(σ k)`, which is the identity.
312	    ext1 σ
313	    simp only [Pi.lift_π]
314	    -- the restriction hom is an endomorphism in the thin `Opensᵒᵖ` category, hence `𝟙`
315	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
316	    -- `erw` is needed: the `Subsingleton.elim` rewrite leaves the term not type-correct
317	    -- under `instances` transparency, so plain `rw [Functor.map_id]` cannot match.
318	    erw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]
319	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
320	    apply Pi.hom_ext
321	    intro σ
322	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
323	    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
324	    congr 1
325	
326	/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
327	(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).
328	
329	The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
330	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
331	obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
332	section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
333	it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
334	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.
335	
336	Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
337	noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
338	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
339	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)

8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the sheaf property and bundled structure sheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (2/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	discharges the sheaf condition for `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 7) and bundles the
13	result as `toModuleKSheaf` (helper 8); the forget-and-recover natural iso to the
14	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf is also packaged here. The downstream
15	finite-length carriers live in the sibling `Carriers.lean`.
16	
17	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
18	-/
19	
20	set_option autoImplicit false
21	
22	universe u v
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
27	
28	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
29	
30	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (7): the presheaf of (6) is a sheaf for the
31	Grothendieck topology of opens of `C.left.toTopCat`. -/
32	lemma toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
33	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
34	      (toModuleKPresheaf C) := by
35	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
36	  convert (Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
37	      (CategoryTheory.forget CommRingCat.{u})).mp C.left.sheaf.property using 1 <;> rfl
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 5 main: the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme as a sheaf
40	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
41	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/
42	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
43	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
44	  ⟨toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C⟩
45	
46	/-- Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007): forget-and-recover natural iso between
47	the iter-006 `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf and the iter-004
48	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf. The two sheaves agree on the nose at
49	the underlying-presheaf level: forgetting from `ModuleCat k` to `AddCommGrpCat`
50	via `forget₂` recovers the iter-004 `toAbSheaf C.left`. Closure body
51	`Iso.refl _`; probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`, iter-007 plan-agent). -/
52	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
53	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
54	    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
55	        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
56	      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
57	  Iso.refl _
58	
59	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
60

4	Authors: Christian Merten

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafAb
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the structure presheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (1/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	ships the categorical gap-fills, the sheafification / `Ext` typeclass prerequisites,
13	and the per-open ring-map helpers (1)–(5) that culminate in the presheaf-of-
14	`k`-modules `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6). Its sibling `SheafProperty.lean`
15	discharges the sheaf condition and bundles `toModuleKSheaf` (helpers 7–8); the
16	sibling `Carriers.lean` carries the downstream finite-length carriers and producer
17	instances.
18	
19	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
20	-/
21	
22	set_option autoImplicit false
23	
24	universe u v
25	
26	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	namespace CategoryTheory
29	
30	universe u₂ v₂
31	
32	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is additive when `D`
33	is preadditive. The `map_add` field reduces to `f + g = f + g` componentwise after
34	`ext; rfl`. -/
35	instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
36	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
37	  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl
38	
39	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear when
40	`D` is `Linear R`-enriched. The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is
41	load-bearing — bare `[Linear R D]` would be parsed as `Linear` of a functor. -/
42	instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
43	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
44	    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
45	    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
46	  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl
47	
48	namespace Adjunction
49	
50	variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
51	    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
52	    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]
53	
54	include adj
55	
56	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
57	then `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear. Mirrors Mathlib's `left_adjoint_additive`; the
58	`simp` call closes via unit naturality + the `Linear.smul_comp` / `Linear.comp_smul`
59	default simp lemmas. -/
60	lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
61	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
62	    -- v4.31.0: the old `simp [adj.homEquiv_unit]` no longer closes; the categorical
63	    -- `Linear.comp_smul`/`smul_comp` only match via `erw` (the `≃ₗ` homModule smul is defeq but
64	    -- not syntactically the `Linear.smul` instance), with unit-naturality in between.
65	    simp only [adj.homEquiv_unit, Functor.map_smul, ← Functor.comp_map]
66	    erw [← adj.unit.naturality, Linear.comp_smul, ← adj.unit.naturality, Linear.smul_comp]
67	    rfl)
68	
69	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
70	then `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear. The dual of `left_adjoint_linear`. -/
71	lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
72	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
73	    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])
74	
75	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): linear lifting of `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`. Under
76	`[F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]`, the additive equivalence
77	`(F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃+ (X ⟶ G.obj Y)` upgrades to a `LinearEquiv` over `R`. The
78	`change` is load-bearing: it exposes the underlying `homEquiv` form, allowing
79	`simp [homEquiv_unit]` to close via the same naturality argument as in
80	`left_adjoint_linear`. -/
81	noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
82	    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
83	  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
84	  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
85	    map_smul' := fun r f => by
86	      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
87	      simp only [adj.homEquiv_unit, Functor.map_smul]
88	      erw [Linear.comp_smul] }
89	
90	end Adjunction
91	
92	end CategoryTheory
93	
94	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
95	
96	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (a): sheafification on the topology of opens of
97	any topological space, valued in `ModuleCat k`. Inferable from Mathlib's
98	small-site / concrete-category sheafification API; the prover's task is the
99	universe pinning, mirroring the iter-004 `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
100	instance instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK
101	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
102	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
103	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
104	  inferInstance
105	
106	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (b): `Ext` on the sheaf category. The universe
107	annotation `HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
108	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`; mirrors the iter-004
109	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
110	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK
111	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
112	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
113	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
114	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
115	  CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _
116	
117	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
118	
119	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
120	
121	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
122	
123	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
124	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
125	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
126	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
127	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
128	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
129	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
130	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
131	
132	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
133	map of `kToSection`. -/
134	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
135	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
136	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
137	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
140	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
141	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
142	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
143	  rfl
144	
145	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
146	natural in `U`. -/
147	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
148	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
149	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
150	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
151	  rfl
152	
153	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
154	`algebraMap`. -/
155	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
156	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
157	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
158	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
159	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
160	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
161	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
162	
163	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
164	
165	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
166	
167	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
168	
169	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
170	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
171	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
172	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
173	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
174	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
175	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
176	      map_add' := fun x y => by
177	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
178	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
179	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
180	        congr 1
181	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
182	          (C := C) f r }
183	  map_id U := by
184	    ext x
185	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
186	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
187	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
188	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
189	    ext x
190	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
191	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
192	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
193	
194	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
195	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
196	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
197	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
198	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
199	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
200	  rfl
201	
202	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
203

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproducts.Cech
10	
11	/-!
12	# Cech cohomology
13	
14	Given a family of objects `U : ι → C` in a category `C` that has finite products,
15	we define a Cech complex functor
16	`cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends a presheaf
17	`P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` in a preadditive category (where products exist) to the cochain
18	complex which in degree `n` consists of the product, indexed by `i : Fin (n + 1) → ι`,
19	of the value of `P` on the product of the objects `U (i a)` for `a : Fin (n + 1)`.
20	
21	-/
22	
23	@[expose] public section
24	
25	universe w t v v' u u'
26	
27	namespace CategoryTheory
28	
29	open Limits
30	
31	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A] [HasProducts.{w} A]
32	
33	namespace Limits.FormalCoproduct
34	
35	open Opposite
36	
37	variable (E : SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C))
38	
39	/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
40	functor `(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CosimplicialObject A` which sends `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
41	cosimplicial object which sends `⦋n⦌` to the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _⦋n⦌`. -/
42	@[simps!]
43	noncomputable def cosimplicialObjectFunctor :
44	    (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CosimplicialObject A :=
45	  evalOp.{w} C A ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj E.rightOp
46	
47	variable [Preadditive A]
48	
49	/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
50	functor `(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
51	cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _⦋n⦌`. -/
52	@[simps!]
53	noncomputable def cochainComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ :=
54	  cosimplicialObjectFunctor E ⋙ AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A
55	
56	end Limits.FormalCoproduct
57	
58	variable [HasFiniteProducts C] [Preadditive A] {ι : Type w} (U : ι → C)
59	
60	/-- Given a family of objects `U : ι → C`, this is the Cech complex functor
61	`(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends a presheaf `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
62	cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the product,
63	indexed by `x : Fin (n + 1) → ι`, of the value of `P` on the product of the
64	objects `U (x i)` for `i : Fin (n + 1)`. -/
65	noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ :=
66	  FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech
67	
68	end CategoryTheory
69

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.SimplicialObject.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproducts.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# The Cech object for formal coproducts
13	
14	Let `C` be a category that has finite products. In this file, we define a
15	functor `cechFunctor : FormalCoproduct C ⥤ SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct C)`
16	which sends a formal coproduct of objects `U j` (for `j : ι`) to the simplicial object
17	which sends `⦋n⦌` to the formal coproduct, indexed by `i : Fin (n + 1) → ι`,
18	of the products of the objects `U (i a)` for all `a : Fin (n + 1)`.
19	
20	-/
21	
22	@[expose] public section
23	
24	universe w t v u
25	
26	namespace CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct
27	
28	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C]
29	
30	/-- Given `U : FormalCoproduct C` and a type `α`, this is the formal coproduct
31	indexed by all `i : α → U.I` of the products of the objects `U.obj (i a)`
32	for all `a : α`. -/
33	@[simps]
34	noncomputable def power (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (α : Type t)
35	    [HasProductsOfShape α C] : FormalCoproduct.{max w t} C where
36	  I := α → U.I
37	  obj i := ∏ᶜ (U.obj ∘ i)
38	
39	section
40	
41	variable (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (α : Type) [HasProductsOfShape α C]
42	
43	variable {α} in
44	/-- The projection `U.power α ⟶ U` for each `a : α`. -/
45	@[simps]
46	noncomputable def powerπ (a : α) : U.power α ⟶ U where
47	  f i := i a
48	  φ _ := Pi.π _ a
49	
50	/-- The (limit) fan expressing that `U.power α` is a product of copies of
51	`U` indexed by `α`. -/
52	noncomputable abbrev powerFan :
53	    Fan (fun (_ : α) ↦ U) :=
54	  Fan.mk (U.power α) U.powerπ
55	
56	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
57	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
58	/-- `U.power α` identifies to the product of copies of `U` indexed by `α`. -/
59	noncomputable def isLimitPowerFan : IsLimit (U.powerFan α) :=
60	  Fan.IsLimit.mk _
61	    (fun s ↦
62	      { f i a := (s.proj a).f i
63	        φ i := Pi.lift (fun a ↦ (s.proj a).φ i) })
64	    (fun _ _ ↦ by ext <;> simp)
65	    (fun s m hm ↦ by
66	      obtain ⟨f, φ⟩ := m
67	      obtain rfl : f = fun i a ↦ (s.proj a).f i := by
68	        ext i
69	        dsimp
70	        ext a
71	        exact congr_fun (congr_arg FormalCoproduct.Hom.f (hm a)) i
72	      ext i
73	      · rfl
74	      · dsimp
75	        ext a
76	        specialize hm a
77	        rw [hom_ext_iff] at hm
78	        obtain ⟨_, hm⟩ := hm
79	        simpa using hm i)
80	
81	end
82	
83	/-- For any morphism `f : U ⟶ V` in `FormalCoproduct C` and a type `α`,
84	this is the induced map `U.power α ⟶ V.power α`. -/
85	@[simps -fullyApplied]
86	noncomputable def powerMap {U V : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f : U ⟶ V) (α : Type t)
87	    [HasProductsOfShape α C] :
88	    U.power α ⟶ V.power α where
89	  f i := f.f ∘ i
90	  φ i := Pi.map (fun a ↦ f.φ (i a))
91	
92	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
93	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
94	@[simp]
95	lemma powerMap_id (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (α : Type t) [HasProductsOfShape α C] :
96	    powerMap (𝟙 U) α = 𝟙 _ := by
97	  cat_disch
98	
99	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
100	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
101	@[reassoc]
102	lemma powerMap_comp {U V W : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f : U ⟶ V) (g : V ⟶ W) (α : Type t)
103	    [HasProductsOfShape α C] :
104	    powerMap (f ≫ g) α = powerMap f α ≫ powerMap g α := by
105	  ext
106	  · cat_disch
107	  · dsimp
108	    ext
109	    simp only [Category.comp_id, Category.assoc, Pi.map_π, Function.comp_apply,
110	      Pi.map_π_assoc]
111	    apply Pi.map_π
112	
113	attribute [local simp] powerMap_comp
114	
115	/-- Given a type `α`, this is the functor `FormalCoproduct C ⥤ FormalCoproduct C`
116	which sends `U` to `U.power α`. -/
117	@[simps]
118	noncomputable def powerFunctor (α : Type t) [HasProductsOfShape α C] :
119	    FormalCoproduct.{w} C ⥤ FormalCoproduct.{max w t} C where
120	  obj U := U.power α
121	  map f := powerMap f α
122	
123	/-- The functoriality of `FormalCoproduct.power` with respect to the index type. -/
124	@[simps -fullyApplied]
125	noncomputable def mapPower (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) {α β : Type t}
126	    [HasProductsOfShape α C] [HasProductsOfShape β C] (f : α → β) :
127	    U.power β ⟶ U.power α where
128	  f i := i ∘ f
129	  φ _ := Pi.lift (fun _ ↦ Pi.π _ _)
130	
131	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
132	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
133	@[simp]
134	lemma mapPower_id (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (α : Type t)
135	    [HasProductsOfShape α C] :
136	    U.mapPower (id : α → α) = 𝟙 _ := by
137	  cat_disch
138	
139	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
140	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
141	@[reassoc]
142	lemma mapPower_comp (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) {α β γ : Type t}
143	    [HasProductsOfShape α C] [HasProductsOfShape β C] [HasProductsOfShape γ C]
144	    (f : α → β) (g : β → γ) :
145	    U.mapPower (g ∘ f) = U.mapPower g ≫ U.mapPower f := by
146	  ext
147	  · cat_disch
148	  · dsimp
149	    ext
150	    simp [Function.comp_def]
151	
152	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
153	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
154	@[reassoc]
155	lemma mapPower_powerMap {U V : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f : U ⟶ V)
156	    {α β : Type t} [HasProductsOfShape α C] [HasProductsOfShape β C] (g : α → β) :
157	    U.mapPower g ≫ powerMap f α = powerMap f β ≫ V.mapPower g := by
158	  ext
159	  · cat_disch
160	  · dsimp
161	    ext
162	    simp [Function.comp_def]
163	
164	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
165	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
166	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
167	lemma mapPower_π (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) {α β : Type}
168	    [HasProductsOfShape α C] [HasProductsOfShape β C] (f : α → β) (a : α) :
169	    mapPower U f ≫ U.powerπ a = U.powerπ (f a) := by
170	  ext <;> simp
171	
172	attribute [local simp] mapPower_comp mapPower_powerMap
173	
174	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
175	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
176	/-- The functor `(Type t)ᵒᵖ ⥤ FormalCoproduct.{w} C ⥤ FormalCoproduct.{max w t} C`
177	which sends a type `α` and `U : FormalCoproduct C` to `U.power α`. -/
178	@[simps]
179	noncomputable def powerBifunctor [HasProducts.{t} C] :
180	    Type tᵒᵖ ⥤ FormalCoproduct.{w} C ⥤ FormalCoproduct.{max w t} C where
181	  obj α := powerFunctor α.unop
182	  map f := { app _ := mapPower _ f.unop }
183	  map_comp _ _ := by ext : 2; simp [types_comp]
184	
185	variable [HasFiniteProducts C]
186	
187	/-- Given `U : FormalCoproduct C`, this is the simplicial object
188	in `FormalCoproduct C` which sends `⦋n⦌` to `U.power (Fin (n + 1))`. -/
189	@[simps]
190	noncomputable def cech (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) :
191	    SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) where
192	  obj n := U.power (ToType n.unop)
193	  map f := U.mapPower f.unop.toOrderHom.toFun
194	
195	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
196	/-- The functor `FormalCoproduct C ⥤ SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct C)`
197	which sends a formal coproduct to its Cech object. -/
198	@[simps]
199	noncomputable def cechFunctor :
200	    FormalCoproduct.{w} C ⥤ SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) where
201	  obj U := U.cech
202	  map f := { app _ := powerMap f _ }
203	  map_comp _ _ := by ext : 1; simp
204	
205	end CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Preserves.Shapes.Products
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Opposites.Products
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Pullback.HasPullback
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Terminal
12	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Pullback.IsPullback.Basic
13	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.ZeroObjects
14	
15	/-!
16	# Formal Coproducts
17	
18	In this file we construct the category of formal coproducts given a category.
19	
20	## Main definitions
21	
22	* `FormalCoproduct`: the category of formal coproducts, which are indexed sets of objects in a
23	  category. A morphism `∐ i : X.I, X.obj i ⟶ ∐ j : Y.I, Y.obj j` is given by a function
24	  `f : X.I → Y.I` and maps `X.obj i ⟶ Y.obj (f i)` for each `i : X.I`.
25	* `FormalCoproduct.eval : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ ((FormalCoproduct C)ᵒᵖ ⥤ A)`:
26	  the universal property that a presheaf on `C` where the target category has arbitrary coproducts,
27	  can be extended to a presheaf on `FormalCoproduct C`.
28	
29	## TODO
30	
31	* `FormalCoproduct.incl C : C ⥤ FormalCoproduct.{w} C` probably preserves every limit?
32	
33	-/
34	
35	@[expose] public section
36	
37	universe w w₁ w₂ w₃ v v₁ v₂ v₃ u u₁ u₂ u₃
38	
39	open Opposite CategoryTheory Functor
40	
41	namespace CategoryTheory
42	
43	namespace Limits
44	
45	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] (A : Type u₁) [Category.{v₁} A]
46	
47	variable (C) in
48	/-- A formal coproduct is an indexed set of objects, where ⟨I, f⟩ corresponds to the "formal
49	coproduct" `⨿ (i : I), f i`, where `f i : C` is the `i`ᵗʰ component. -/
50	structure FormalCoproduct where
51	  /-- The indexing type. -/
52	  I : Type w
53	  /-- The object in the original category indexed by `x : I`. -/
54	  obj (i : I) : C
55	
56	namespace FormalCoproduct
57	
58	/-- A morphism `(⨿ (i : X.I), X.obj i) ⟶ (⨿ (j : Y.I), Y.obj i)` is given by first a function
59	on the indexing sets `f : X.I → Y.I`, and then for each `i : X.I` a morphism
60	`X.obj i ⟶ Y.obj (f i)`. -/
61	structure Hom (X Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) where
62	  /-- The function on the indexing sets. -/
63	  f : X.I → Y.I
64	  /-- The map on each component. -/
65	  φ (i : X.I) : X.obj i ⟶ Y.obj (f i)
66	
67	-- this category identifies to the full subcategory of the category of
68	-- presheaves of sets on `C` which are coproducts of representable presheaves
69	@[simps!] instance category : Category (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) where
70	  Hom := Hom
71	  id X := { f := id, φ := fun _ ↦ 𝟙 _ }
72	  comp α β := { f := β.f ∘ α.f, φ := fun _ ↦ α.φ _ ≫ β.φ _ }
73	
74	@[ext (iff := false)]
75	lemma hom_ext {X Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} {f g : X ⟶ Y} (h₁ : f.f = g.f)
76	    (h₂ : ∀ (i : X.I), f.φ i ≫ eqToHom (by rw [h₁]) = g.φ i) : f = g := by
77	  obtain ⟨f, F⟩ := f
78	  obtain ⟨g, G⟩ := g
79	  obtain rfl : f = g := h₁
80	  obtain rfl : F = G := by ext i; simpa using h₂ i
81	  rfl
82	
83	lemma hom_ext_iff {X Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f g : X ⟶ Y) :
84	    f = g ↔ ∃ h₁ : f.f = g.f, ∀ (i : X.I), f.φ i ≫ eqToHom (by rw [h₁]) = g.φ i :=
85	  ⟨(· ▸ by simp), fun ⟨h₁, h₂⟩ ↦ hom_ext h₁ h₂⟩
86	
87	lemma hom_ext_iff' {X Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f g : X ⟶ Y) :
88	    f = g ↔ ∀ i : X.I, ∃ h₁ : f.f i = g.f i, f.φ i ≫ eqToHom (by rw [h₁]) = g.φ i :=
89	  ⟨(· ▸ by simp), fun h ↦ hom_ext (funext fun i ↦ (h i).fst) fun i ↦ (h i).snd⟩
90	
91	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
92	/-- A way to create isomorphisms in the category of formal coproducts, by creating an `Equiv`
93	between the indexing sets, and then correspondingly isomorphisms of each component. -/
94	@[simps!] def isoOfComponents {X Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (e : X.I ≃ Y.I)
95	    (h : ∀ i, X.obj i ≅ Y.obj (e i)) : X ≅ Y where
96	  hom := { f := e, φ := fun i ↦ (h i).hom }
97	  inv := { f := e.symm, φ := fun i ↦ eqToHom (by simp) ≫ (h (e.symm i)).inv }
98	  hom_inv_id := by ext <;> aesop
99	  inv_hom_id := by ext <;> aesop
100	
101	variable (C) in
102	/-- An object of the original category produces a formal coproduct on that object only, so indexed
103	by `PUnit`, the type with one element. -/
104	@[simps!] def incl : C ⥤ FormalCoproduct.{w} C where
105	  obj X := ⟨PUnit, fun _ ↦ X⟩
106	  map f := ⟨fun _ ↦ PUnit.unit, fun _ ↦ f⟩
107	
108	section fromIncl
109	
110	variable {X : C} {Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C}
111	
112	/-- A map `incl(X) ⟶ Y` is specified by an element of `Y`'s indexing set, and then a morphism
113	`X ⟶ Y.obj i` in the original category. -/
114	@[simps!] def Hom.fromIncl (i : Y.I) (f : X ⟶ Y.obj i) : (incl C).obj X ⟶ Y :=
115	  ⟨fun _ ↦ i, fun _ ↦ f⟩
116	
117	/-- A map `incl(X) ⟶ Y` is specified by an element of `Y`'s indexing set, and then a morphism
118	`X ⟶ Y.obj i` in the original category. -/
119	def Hom.asSigma (f : (incl C).obj X ⟶ Y) : Σ (i : Y.I), X ⟶ Y.obj i :=
120	  ⟨f.f PUnit.unit, f.φ PUnit.unit⟩
121	
122	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
123	lemma Hom.fromIncl_asSigma (f : (incl C).obj X ⟶ Y) :
124	    Hom.fromIncl f.asSigma.fst f.asSigma.snd = f := by
125	  ext <;> aesop
126	
127	end fromIncl
128	
129	-- This is probably some form of adjunction?
130	/-- A map `incl(X) ⟶ Y` is specified by an element of `Y`'s indexing set, and then a morphism
131	`X ⟶ Y.obj i` in the original category. -/
132	@[simps!] def inclHomEquiv (X : C) (Y : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) :
133	    ((incl C).obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ (i : Y.I) × (X ⟶ Y.obj i) where
134	  toFun f := f.asSigma
135	  invFun f := .fromIncl f.1 f.2
136	  left_inv f := f.fromIncl_asSigma
137	  right_inv _ := rfl
138	
139	/-- `incl` is fully faithful, which means that `(X ⟶ Y) ≃ (incl(X) ⟶ incl(Y))`. -/
140	@[simps!] def fullyFaithfulIncl : (incl C).FullyFaithful where
141	  preimage f := f.φ PUnit.unit
142	
143	instance : (incl C).Full :=
144	  fullyFaithfulIncl.full
145	
146	instance : (incl C).Faithful :=
147	  fullyFaithfulIncl.faithful
148	
149	/-- A family of maps with the same target can be turned into one arrow in the category of formal
150	coproducts. This is used in Čech cohomology. -/
151	@[simps!] def homOfPiHom (X : C) {J : Type w} (f : (j : J) → C) (φ : (j : J) → f j ⟶ X) :
152	    FormalCoproduct.mk _ f ⟶ (incl C).obj X :=
153	  ⟨fun _ ↦ PUnit.unit, φ⟩
154	
155	section Coproduct
156	
157	variable (𝒜 : Type w) (f : 𝒜 → FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (t X : FormalCoproduct.{w} C)
158	
159	/-- We construct explicitly the data that specify the coproduct of a given family of formal
160	coproducts. -/
161	def cofan : Cofan f :=
162	  Cofan.mk ⟨(i : 𝒜) × (f i).I, fun p ↦ (f p.1).obj p.2⟩
163	    fun i ↦ ⟨fun x ↦ ⟨i, x⟩, fun x ↦ 𝟙 ((f i).obj x)⟩
164	
165	section simp_lemmas
166	
167	variable {𝒜 f}
168	
169	theorem cofan_inj (i : 𝒜) : (cofan 𝒜 f).inj i = ⟨fun x ↦ ⟨i, x⟩, fun x ↦ 𝟙 ((f i).obj x)⟩ := rfl
170	
171	@[simp] lemma cofan_inj_f_fst (i : 𝒜) (x) : (((cofan 𝒜 f).inj i).f x).1 = i := rfl
172	
173	@[simp] lemma cofan_inj_f_snd (i : 𝒜) (x) : (((cofan 𝒜 f).inj i).f x).2 = x := rfl
174	
175	@[simp] lemma cofan_inj_φ (i : 𝒜) (x) : ((cofan 𝒜 f).inj i).φ x = 𝟙 ((f i).obj x) := rfl
176	
177	end simp_lemmas
178	
179	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
180	/-- The explicit `Equiv` between maps from the constructed coproduct `cofan 𝒜 f` and families of
181	maps from each component, which is the universal property of coproducts. -/
182	@[simps!] def cofanHomEquiv :
183	    ((cofan 𝒜 f).pt ⟶ t) ≃ ((i : 𝒜) → (f i ⟶ t)) where
184	  toFun m i := (cofan 𝒜 f).inj i ≫ m
185	  invFun s := ⟨fun p ↦ (s p.1).f p.2, fun p ↦ (s p.1).φ p.2⟩
186	  left_inv m := hom_ext rfl (fun ⟨i, x⟩ ↦ by simp [cofan_inj])
187	  right_inv p := by ext <;> simp
188	
189	/-- `cofan 𝒜 f` is a coproduct of `f`. -/
190	@[simps!] def isColimitCofan : IsColimit (cofan 𝒜 f) :=
191	  Cofan.IsColimit.mk (cofan 𝒜 f) (fun t ↦ (cofanHomEquiv _ _ _).symm t.inj)
192	    (fun t i ↦ congrFun ((cofanHomEquiv _ _ _).right_inv t.inj) i)
193	    (fun _ _ h ↦ (Equiv.eq_symm_apply _).2 (funext h))
194	
195	instance : HasCoproducts.{w} (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) :=
196	  hasCoproducts_of_colimit_cofans _ (isColimitCofan _)
197	
198	/-- The arbitrary choice of the coproduct is isomorphic to our constructed coproduct `cofan 𝒜 f`.
199	-/
200	noncomputable def coproductIsoCofanPt : ∐ f ≅ (cofan 𝒜 f).pt :=
201	  colimit.isoColimitCocone ⟨_, isColimitCofan _ _⟩
202	
203	variable {𝒜 f} in
204	@[reassoc (attr := simp)] lemma ι_comp_coproductIsoCofanPt (i) :
205	    Sigma.ι f i ≫ (coproductIsoCofanPt 𝒜 f).hom = (cofan 𝒜 f).inj i :=
206	  colimit.isoColimitCocone_ι_hom _ _
207	
208	/-- Each `X : FormalCoproduct.{w} C` is actually itself a coproduct of objects of the original
209	category (after coercion using `incl C`). This is the function that specifies the family for which
210	`X` is a coproduct of. -/
211	def toFun (X : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) : X.I → FormalCoproduct.{w} C :=
212	  (incl C).obj ∘ X.obj
213	
214	/-- The witness that each `X : FormalCoproduct.{w} C` is itself a coproduct of objects of the
215	original category (after coercion using `incl C`), specified by `X.toFun`. -/
216	def cofanPtIsoSelf : (cofan X.I X.toFun).pt ≅ X :=
217	  isoOfComponents (Equiv.sigmaPUnit X.I) fun i ↦ Iso.refl (X.obj i.fst)
218	
219	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
220	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
221	lemma inj_comp_cofanPtIsoSelf_hom (i : X.I) :
222	    (cofan X.I X.toFun).inj i ≫ (cofanPtIsoSelf X).hom = .fromIncl i (𝟙 (X.obj i)) :=
223	  hom_ext rfl (fun i => by aesop)
224	
225	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
226	lemma fromIncl_comp_cofanPtIsoSelf_inv (i : X.I) :
227	    Hom.fromIncl i (𝟙 (X.obj i)) ≫ (cofanPtIsoSelf X).inv = (cofan X.I X.toFun).inj i :=
228	  (Iso.comp_inv_eq _).2 (inj_comp_cofanPtIsoSelf_hom _ _).symm
229	
230	/-- The isomorphism between the coproduct of `X.toFun` and the object `X` itself. -/
231	@[simps!] noncomputable def coproductIsoSelf :
232	    ∐ X.toFun ≅ X :=
233	  coproductIsoCofanPt _ _ ≪≫ cofanPtIsoSelf X
234	
235	@[reassoc (attr := simp)] lemma ι_comp_coproductIsoSelf_hom (i : X.I) :
236	    Sigma.ι _ i ≫ (coproductIsoSelf X).hom = .fromIncl i (𝟙 (X.obj i)) := by
237	  simp [coproductIsoSelf]
238	
239	@[reassoc (attr := simp)] lemma fromIncl_comp_coproductIsoSelf_inv (i : X.I) :
240	    Hom.fromIncl i (𝟙 (X.obj i)) ≫ (coproductIsoSelf X).inv = Sigma.ι X.toFun i :=
241	  (Iso.comp_inv_eq _).2 (ι_comp_coproductIsoSelf_hom _ _).symm
242	
243	end Coproduct
244	
245	section Terminal
246	
247	/-- Given a terminal object `T` in the original category, we show that `incl(T)` is a terminal
248	object in the category of formal coproducts. -/
249	def isTerminalIncl (T : C) (ht : IsTerminal T) : IsTerminal ((incl C).obj T) :=
250	  IsTerminal.ofUniqueHom (fun _ ↦ ⟨fun _ ↦ PUnit.unit, fun _ ↦ ht.from _⟩)
251	    (fun _ _ ↦ hom_ext (funext fun _ ↦ rfl) (fun _ ↦ ht.hom_ext _ _))
252	
253	instance [HasTerminal C] : HasTerminal (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) :=
254	  (isTerminalIncl (⊤_ C) terminalIsTerminal).hasTerminal
255	
256	end Terminal
257	
258	section Pullback
259	
260	variable {X Y Z : FormalCoproduct.{w} C} (f : X ⟶ Z) (g : Y ⟶ Z)
261	  (pb : ∀ i : Function.Pullback f.f g.f,
262	    PullbackCone (f.φ i.1.1 ≫ eqToHom (by rw [i.2])) (g.φ i.1.2))
263	  (hpb : ∀ i, IsLimit (pb i))
264	  (T : FormalCoproduct.{w} C)
265	
266	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
267	/-- Given two morphisms `f : X ⟶ Z` and `g : Y ⟶ Z`, given pullback in `C` over each component,
268	construct the pullback in `FormalCategory.{w} C`. -/
269	def pullbackCone : PullbackCone f g :=
270	  .mk (W := ⟨Function.Pullback f.f g.f, fun i ↦ (pb i).pt⟩)
271	    ⟨fun i ↦ i.1.fst, fun i ↦ (pb i).fst⟩
272	    ⟨fun i ↦ i.1.snd, fun i ↦ (pb i).snd⟩
273	    (hom_ext (funext fun i ↦ i.2) (fun i ↦ by simp [(pb i).condition]))
274	
275	section simp_lemmas
276	
277	@[simp] lemma pullbackCone_fst_f (i) : (pullbackCone f g pb).fst.f i = i.1.1 := rfl
278	
279	@[simp] lemma pullbackCone_fst_φ (i) : (pullbackCone f g pb).fst.φ i = (pb i).fst := rfl
280	
281	@[simp] lemma pullbackCone_snd_f (i) : (pullbackCone f g pb).snd.f i = i.1.2 := rfl
282	
283	@[simp] lemma pullbackCone_snd_φ (i) : (pullbackCone f g pb).snd.φ i = (pb i).snd := rfl
284	
285	@[simp] lemma pullbackCone_condition :
286	    (pullbackCone f g pb).fst ≫ f = (pullbackCone f g pb).snd ≫ g :=
287	  PullbackCone.condition _
288	
289	end simp_lemmas
290	
291	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
292	/-- The `Equiv` that witnesses that `pullbackCone f g pb` is actually a pullback. This is the
293	universal property of pullbacks. -/
294	@[simps!] def homPullbackEquiv : (T ⟶ (pullbackCone f g pb).pt) ≃
295	    { p : (T ⟶ X) × (T ⟶ Y) // p.1 ≫ f = p.2 ≫ g } where
296	  toFun m := ⟨⟨m ≫ (pullbackCone f g pb).fst, m ≫ (pullbackCone f g pb).snd⟩, by simp⟩
297	  invFun s := ⟨fun i ↦ ⟨(s.1.1.f i, s.1.2.f i), congrFun (congrArg Hom.f s.2) i⟩,
298	    fun i ↦ (hpb _).lift (PullbackCone.mk (s.1.1.φ i) (s.1.2.φ i)
299	      (by simpa using ((hom_ext_iff _ _).1 s.2).2 i))⟩
300	  left_inv m := hom_ext rfl (fun i ↦ by
301	    simp only [eqToHom_refl, Category.comp_id]
302	    exact (hpb _).hom_ext ((pb _).equalizer_ext (by aesop) (by aesop)))
303	  right_inv s := by ext <;> simp
304	
305	/-- `pullbackCone f g pb` is a pullback. -/
306	def isLimitPullbackCone : IsLimit (pullbackCone f g pb) := by
307	  refine PullbackCone.IsLimit.mk
308	    (fst := (pullbackCone f g pb).fst) (snd := (pullbackCone f g pb).snd) _
309	    (fun s ↦ (homPullbackEquiv f g pb hpb s.pt).2 ⟨(s.fst, s.snd), s.condition⟩)
310	    (fun s ↦ congrArg (·.1.fst)
311	      ((homPullbackEquiv f g pb hpb s.pt).right_inv ⟨(s.fst, s.snd), s.condition⟩))
312	    (fun s ↦ congrArg (·.1.snd)
313	      ((homPullbackEquiv f g pb hpb s.pt).right_inv ⟨(s.fst, s.snd), s.condition⟩))
314	    (fun s m h₁ h₂ ↦ ?_)
315	  convert! ((homPullbackEquiv f g pb hpb s.pt).left_inv m).symm using 3
316	  rw [← h₁, ← h₂]; rfl
317	
318	-- Arguments cannot be inferred.
319	include pb hpb in
320	theorem hasPullback_of_pullbackCone : HasPullback f g :=
321	  ⟨⟨⟨_, isLimitPullbackCone f g pb hpb⟩⟩⟩
322	
323	include hpb in
324	lemma isPullback : IsPullback (pullbackCone f g pb).fst (pullbackCone f g pb).snd f g :=
325	  ⟨⟨pullbackCone_condition f g pb⟩, ⟨isLimitPullbackCone f g pb hpb⟩⟩
326	
327	omit pb
328	variable [HasPullbacks C]
329	
330	instance : HasPullback f g :=
331	  hasPullback_of_pullbackCone f g (fun _ ↦ pullback.cone _ _) (fun _ ↦ pullback.isLimit _ _)
332	
333	instance : HasPullbacks (FormalCoproduct.{w} C) :=
334	  hasPullbacks_of_hasLimit_cospan _
335	
336	end Pullback
337	
338	noncomputable section HasCoproducts
339	
340	variable [HasCoproducts.{w} A] (C) (J : Type w) (f : J → FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (F : C ⥤ A)
341	
342	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
343	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
344	/-- A copresheaf valued in a category `A` with arbitrary coproducts, can be extended to the category
345	of formal coproducts. -/
346	@[simps!] def eval : (C ⥤ A) ⥤ (FormalCoproduct.{w} C ⥤ A) where
347	  obj F :=
348	    { obj X := ∐ fun (i : X.I) ↦ F.obj (X.obj i)
349	      map {X Y} f := Sigma.desc fun i ↦ F.map (f.φ i) ≫ Sigma.ι (F.obj ∘ Y.obj) (f.f i)
350	      map_comp _ _ := Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun _ ↦ by simp [Sigma.ι_desc]) }
351	  map α := { app f := Sigma.map fun i ↦ α.app (f.obj i) }
352	
353	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
354	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
355	/-- `eval(F)` restricted to the original category (via `incl`) is the original copresheaf `F`. -/
356	@[simps!] def evalCompInclIsoId :
357	    eval C A ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ A).obj (incl C) ≅ Functor.id (C ⥤ A) :=
358	  NatIso.ofComponents fun F ↦ NatIso.ofComponents
359	    (fun x ↦ ⟨Sigma.desc fun _ ↦ 𝟙 _, Sigma.ι (fun _ ↦ F.obj x) PUnit.unit, by aesop, by simp⟩)
360	    (fun f ↦ Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (by simp [Sigma.ι_desc]))
361	
362	variable {C A}
363	
364	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
365	/-- `eval(F)` preserves arbitrary coproducts. -/
366	def isColimitEvalMapCoconeCofan : IsColimit (((eval.{w} C A).obj F).mapCocone (cofan.{w} J f)) where
367	  desc s := Sigma.desc fun i ↦ Sigma.ι (F.obj ∘ (f i.1).obj) i.2 ≫ s.ι.app ⟨i.1⟩
368	  fac s i := Sigma.hom_ext _ _ fun i ↦ by simp [cofan, Function.comp_def]
369	  uniq s m h := Sigma.hom_ext _ _ fun ⟨i₁, i₂⟩ ↦ by simp [← h, cofan, Function.comp_def]
370	
371	instance : PreservesColimit (Discrete.functor f) ((eval.{w} C A).obj F) :=
372	  ⟨fun hc ↦ ⟨IsColimit.ofIsoColimit (isColimitEvalMapCoconeCofan J f F)
373	    ((Cocone.functoriality _ _).mapIso ((isColimitCofan J f).uniqueUpToIso hc))⟩⟩
374	
375	instance : PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete J) ((eval.{w} C A).obj F) :=
376	  preservesColimitsOfShape_of_discrete _
377	
378	end HasCoproducts
379	
380	noncomputable section HasProducts
381	
382	variable [HasProducts.{w} A] (C) (J : Type w) (f : J → FormalCoproduct.{w} C) (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A)
383	
384	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
385	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
386	/-- A presheaf valued in a category `A` with arbitrary products can be extended to the category of
387	formal coproducts. -/
388	@[simps!] def evalOp : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ ((FormalCoproduct.{w} C)ᵒᵖ ⥤ A) where
389	  obj F :=
390	    { obj X := ∏ᶜ fun (i : X.unop.I) ↦ F.obj (op (X.unop.obj i))
391	      map f := Pi.lift fun i ↦ Pi.π _ (f.unop.f i) ≫ F.map (f.unop.φ i).op }
392	  map α := { app f := Pi.map fun i ↦ α.app (op (f.unop.obj i)) }
393	
394	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
395	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
396	/-- `evalOp(F)` restricted to the original category (via `incl`) is the original presheaf `F`. -/
397	@[simps!] def evalOpCompInlIsoId :
398	    evalOp C A ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ A).obj (incl C).op ≅ Functor.id (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) :=
399	  NatIso.ofComponents fun F ↦ NatIso.ofComponents fun x ↦
400	    ⟨Pi.π _ PUnit.unit, Pi.lift fun _ ↦ 𝟙 _, by aesop, by simp⟩
401	
402	variable {C A}
403	
404	set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
405	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
406	/-- `evalOp(F)` preserves arbitrary products. -/
407	def isLimitEvalMapConeCofanOp : IsLimit (((evalOp.{w} C A).obj F).mapCone (cofan.{w} J f).op) where
408	  lift s := Pi.lift fun i ↦ s.π.app ⟨i.1⟩ ≫ Pi.π _ i.2
409	  fac s i := Pi.hom_ext _ _ fun i ↦ by simp [cofan]
410	  uniq s m h := Pi.hom_ext _ _ fun ⟨i₁, i₂⟩ ↦ by simp [← h, cofan]
411	
412	instance : PreservesLimit (Discrete.functor (op ∘ f)) ((evalOp.{w} C A).obj F) :=
413	  ⟨fun hc ↦ ⟨IsLimit.ofIsoLimit (isLimitEvalMapConeCofanOp J f F) ((Cone.functoriality _ _).mapIso
414	    ((Cofan.IsColimit.op (isColimitCofan J f)).uniqueUpToIso hc))⟩⟩
415	
416	end HasProducts
417	
418	end FormalCoproduct
419	
420	end Limits
421	
422	end CategoryTheory

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2019 Kim Morrison. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Kim Morrison, Justus Springer
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Category.Preorder
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.FiniteLimits
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.Products
11	public import Mathlib.Data.Finset.Lattice.Fold
12	
13	/-!
14	# Limits in lattice categories are given by infimums and supremums.
15	-/
16	
17	@[expose] public section
18	
19	
20	universe w w' u
21	
22	namespace CategoryTheory.Limits.CompleteLattice
23	
24	section Semilattice
25	
26	variable {α : Type u} {J : Type w} [SmallCategory J] [FinCategory J]
27	
28	/-- The limit cone over any functor from a finite diagram into a `SemilatticeInf` with `OrderTop`.
29	-/
30	@[simps]
31	def finiteLimitCone [SemilatticeInf α] [OrderTop α] (F : J ⥤ α) : LimitCone F where
32	  cone :=
33	    { pt := Finset.univ.inf F.obj
34	      π := { app := fun _ => homOfLE (Finset.inf_le (Fintype.complete _)) } }
35	  isLimit := { lift := fun s => homOfLE (Finset.le_inf fun j _ => (s.π.app j).down.down) }
36	
37	/--
38	The colimit cocone over any functor from a finite diagram into a `SemilatticeSup` with `OrderBot`.
39	-/
40	@[simps]
41	def finiteColimitCocone [SemilatticeSup α] [OrderBot α] (F : J ⥤ α) : ColimitCocone F where
42	  cocone :=
43	    { pt := Finset.univ.sup F.obj
44	      ι := { app := fun _ => homOfLE (Finset.le_sup (Fintype.complete _)) } }
45	  isColimit := { desc := fun s => homOfLE (Finset.sup_le fun j _ => (s.ι.app j).down.down) }
46	
47	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
48	instance (priority := 100) hasFiniteLimits_of_semilatticeInf_orderTop [SemilatticeInf α]
49	    [OrderTop α] : HasFiniteLimits α := ⟨by
50	  intro J 𝒥₁ 𝒥₂
51	  exact { has_limit := fun F => HasLimit.mk (finiteLimitCone F) }⟩
52	
53	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
54	instance (priority := 100) hasFiniteColimits_of_semilatticeSup_orderBot [SemilatticeSup α]
55	    [OrderBot α] : HasFiniteColimits α := ⟨by
56	  intro J 𝒥₁ 𝒥₂
57	  exact { has_colimit := fun F => HasColimit.mk (finiteColimitCocone F) }⟩
58	
59	/-- The limit of a functor from a finite diagram into a `SemilatticeInf` with `OrderTop` is the
60	infimum of the objects in the image.
61	-/
62	theorem finite_limit_eq_finset_univ_inf [SemilatticeInf α] [OrderTop α] (F : J ⥤ α) :
63	    limit F = Finset.univ.inf F.obj :=
64	  (IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso (limit.isLimit F) (finiteLimitCone F).isLimit).to_eq
65	
66	/-- The colimit of a functor from a finite diagram into a `SemilatticeSup` with `OrderBot`
67	is the supremum of the objects in the image.
68	-/
69	theorem finite_colimit_eq_finset_univ_sup [SemilatticeSup α] [OrderBot α] (F : J ⥤ α) :
70	    colimit F = Finset.univ.sup F.obj :=
71	  (IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit F) (finiteColimitCocone F).isColimit).to_eq
72	
73	/--
74	A finite product in the category of a `SemilatticeInf` with `OrderTop` is the same as the infimum.
75	-/
76	theorem finite_product_eq_finset_inf [SemilatticeInf α] [OrderTop α] {ι : Type u} [Fintype ι]
77	    (f : ι → α) : ∏ᶜ f = Finset.univ.inf f := by
78	  trans
79	  · exact
80	      (IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso (limit.isLimit _)
81	          (finiteLimitCone (Discrete.functor f)).isLimit).to_eq
82	  change Finset.univ.inf (f ∘ discreteEquiv.toEmbedding) = Fintype.elems.inf f
83	  simp only [← Finset.inf_map, Finset.univ_map_equiv_to_embedding]
84	  rfl
85	
86	/-- A finite coproduct in the category of a `SemilatticeSup` with `OrderBot` is the same as the
87	supremum.
88	-/
89	theorem finite_coproduct_eq_finset_sup [SemilatticeSup α] [OrderBot α] {ι : Type u} [Fintype ι]
90	    (f : ι → α) : ∐ f = Finset.univ.sup f := by
91	  trans
92	  · exact
93	      (IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit _)
94	          (finiteColimitCocone (Discrete.functor f)).isColimit).to_eq
95	  change Finset.univ.sup (f ∘ discreteEquiv.toEmbedding) = Fintype.elems.sup f
96	  simp only [← Finset.sup_map, Finset.univ_map_equiv_to_embedding]
97	  rfl
98	
99	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
100	instance (priority := 100) [SemilatticeInf α] [OrderTop α] : HasBinaryProducts α := by
101	  have : ∀ x y : α, HasLimit (pair x y) := by
102	    letI := hasFiniteLimits_of_hasFiniteLimits_of_size.{u} α
103	    infer_instance
104	  apply hasBinaryProducts_of_hasLimit_pair
105	
106	/-- The binary product in the category of a `SemilatticeInf` with `OrderTop` is the same as the
107	infimum.
108	-/
109	@[simp]
110	theorem prod_eq_inf [SemilatticeInf α] [OrderTop α] (x y : α) : Limits.prod x y = x ⊓ y :=
111	  calc
112	    Limits.prod x y = limit (pair x y) := rfl
113	    _ = Finset.univ.inf (pair x y).obj := by rw [finite_limit_eq_finset_univ_inf (pair.{u} x y)]
114	    _ = x ⊓ (y ⊓ ⊤) := rfl
115	    -- Note: finset.inf is realized as a fold, hence the definitional equality
116	    _ = x ⊓ y := by rw [inf_top_eq]
117	
118	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
119	instance (priority := 100) [SemilatticeSup α] [OrderBot α] : HasBinaryCoproducts α := by
120	  have : ∀ x y : α, HasColimit (pair x y) := by
121	    letI := hasFiniteColimits_of_hasFiniteColimits_of_size.{u} α
122	    infer_instance
123	  apply hasBinaryCoproducts_of_hasColimit_pair
124	
125	/-- The binary coproduct in the category of a `SemilatticeSup` with `OrderBot` is the same as the
126	supremum.
127	-/
128	@[simp]
129	theorem coprod_eq_sup [SemilatticeSup α] [OrderBot α] (x y : α) : Limits.coprod x y = x ⊔ y :=
130	  calc
131	    Limits.coprod x y = colimit (pair x y) := rfl
132	    _ = Finset.univ.sup (pair x y).obj := by rw [finite_colimit_eq_finset_univ_sup (pair x y)]
133	    _ = x ⊔ (y ⊔ ⊥) := rfl
134	    -- Note: Finset.sup is realized as a fold, hence the definitional equality
135	    _ = x ⊔ y := by rw [sup_bot_eq]
136	
137	/-- The pullback in the category of a `SemilatticeInf` with `OrderTop` is the same as the infimum
138	over the objects.
139	-/
140	@[simp]
141	theorem pullback_eq_inf [SemilatticeInf α] [OrderTop α] {x y z : α} (f : x ⟶ z) (g : y ⟶ z) :
142	    pullback f g = x ⊓ y :=
143	  calc
144	    pullback f g = limit (cospan f g) := rfl
145	    _ = Finset.univ.inf (cospan f g).obj := by rw [finite_limit_eq_finset_univ_inf]
146	    _ = z ⊓ (x ⊓ (y ⊓ ⊤)) := rfl
147	    _ = z ⊓ (x ⊓ y) := by rw [inf_top_eq]
148	    _ = x ⊓ y := inf_eq_right.mpr (inf_le_of_left_le f.le)
149	
150	/-- The pushout in the category of a `SemilatticeSup` with `OrderBot` is the same as the supremum
151	over the objects.
152	-/
153	@[simp]
154	theorem pushout_eq_sup [SemilatticeSup α] [OrderBot α] (x y z : α) (f : z ⟶ x) (g : z ⟶ y) :
155	    pushout f g = x ⊔ y :=
156	  calc
157	    pushout f g = colimit (span f g) := rfl
158	    _ = Finset.univ.sup (span f g).obj := by rw [finite_colimit_eq_finset_univ_sup]
159	    _ = z ⊔ (x ⊔ (y ⊔ ⊥)) := rfl
160	    _ = z ⊔ (x ⊔ y) := by rw [sup_bot_eq]
161	    _ = x ⊔ y := sup_eq_right.mpr (le_sup_of_le_left f.le)
162	
163	end Semilattice
164	
165	variable {α : Type u} [CompleteLattice α] {J : Type w} [Category.{w'} J]
166	
167	/-- The limit cone over any functor into a complete lattice.
168	-/
169	@[simps]
170	def limitCone (F : J ⥤ α) : LimitCone F where
171	  cone :=
172	    { pt := iInf F.obj
173	      π := { app := fun _ => homOfLE (sInf_le (Set.mem_range_self _)) } }
174	  isLimit :=
175	    { lift := fun s =>
176	        homOfLE (le_sInf (by rintro _ ⟨j, rfl⟩; exact (s.π.app j).le)) }
177	
178	/-- The colimit cocone over any functor into a complete lattice.
179	-/
180	@[simps]
181	def colimitCocone (F : J ⥤ α) : ColimitCocone F where
182	  cocone :=
183	    { pt := iSup F.obj
184	      ι := { app := fun _ => homOfLE (le_sSup (Set.mem_range_self _)) } }
185	  isColimit :=
186	    { desc := fun s =>
187	        homOfLE (sSup_le (by rintro _ ⟨j, rfl⟩; exact (s.ι.app j).le)) }
188	
189	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
190	instance (priority := 100) hasLimits_of_completeLattice : HasLimitsOfSize.{w, w'} α where
191	  has_limits_of_shape _ := { has_limit := fun F => HasLimit.mk (limitCone F) }
192	
193	-- see Note [lower instance priority]
194	instance (priority := 100) hasColimits_of_completeLattice : HasColimitsOfSize.{w, w'} α where
195	  has_colimits_of_shape _ := { has_colimit := fun F => HasColimit.mk (colimitCocone F) }
196	
197	/-- The limit of a functor into a complete lattice is the infimum of the objects in the image.
198	-/
199	theorem limit_eq_iInf (F : J ⥤ α) : limit F = iInf F.obj :=
200	  (IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso (limit.isLimit F) (limitCone F).isLimit).to_eq
201	
202	/-- The colimit of a functor into a complete lattice is the supremum of the objects in the image.
203	-/
204	theorem colimit_eq_iSup (F : J ⥤ α) : colimit F = iSup F.obj :=
205	  (IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit F) (colimitCocone F).isColimit).to_eq
206	
207	end CategoryTheory.Limits.CompleteLattice
