9	/-!
10	# Serre vanishing on affines (Stacks 02KG) — affine cover-system infrastructure
11	
12	Project-local: builds the *affine cover system* feeding the basis-comparison criterion
13	`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO). The basis `B` is the distinguished opens `D(f)`
14	of an affine scheme, and the admissible coverings `Cov` are the standard open covers.
15	
16	This file builds the cover-system infrastructure (Lane 1 of the 02KG decomposition) as far
17	as possible; the quasi-coherent seed `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh` and the top
18	`affine_serre_vanishing` are handed off to the assembly iteration (they consume Lane 2's
19	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`).
20	-/
21	
22	universe u
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	-- Re-activate the (file-local) `HasExt` instance from `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` so that the
29	-- `Ext`-based absolute cohomology resolves here without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom` search.
30	attribute [local instance] hasExtModules
31	
32	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
33	
34	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — distinguished opens closed under finite intersection -/
35	
36	/-- **Distinguished opens are closed under finite intersection** (Stacks 02KG, condition (1)).
37	For a family `s : ι → R` of ring elements and a Čech multi-index `σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι`, the
38	`(p + 1)`-fold intersection of distinguished opens `⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k})` is again a distinguished
39	open, namely `D(∏ₖ s_{σ k})`; hence it lies in the basis of distinguished opens. This discharges
40	the `faces_mem` field of the affine cover system. Project-local: re-export of `basicOpen_sprod`
41	in the membership shape the `BasisCovSystem.faces_mem` field consumes. -/
42	theorem affine_faces_mem {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} (s : ι → R)
43	    {p : ℕ} (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
44	    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
45	      ∈ Set.range (fun f : R => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)) :=
46	  ⟨∏ k, s (σ k), (basicOpen_sprod (p + 1) s σ).symm⟩
47	
48	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — covering-datum bridge to the open-cover Čech form -/
49	
50	/-- **The standard affine open cover realizes the distinguished-open family.** For a spanning
51	family `s : ι → R`, the `i`-th member of the standard affine open cover
52	`affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs` of `Spec R` has open range exactly the distinguished open
53	`D(s_i)`, i.e. `coverOpen 𝒰 i = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)`. This is the open-level half of the
54	covering-datum bridge (`lem:cover_datum_bridge`): funext-rewriting the raw family `c.2 = fun i ↦
55	D(s_i)` to `coverOpen 𝒰` identifies the two section Čech complexes, so the Čech cohomology computed
56	over the raw `CovDatum` and over the `X.OpenCover` agree. Project-local: needed to feed the
57	`X.OpenCover`-shaped `injective_cech_acyclic` into the raw-family `BasisCovSystem` fields. -/
58	theorem coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} [Finite ι]
59	    (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (i : ι) :
60	    coverOpen (Scheme.AffineOpenCover.openCover (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs)) i

130	  intro K _ _
131	  constructor
132	  intro F
133	  set D := F ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget R with hD
134	  have e : F ≅ D ⋙ PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj) :=
135	    Functor.isoWhiskerLeft F
136	      (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)).counit).symm
137	  have hc : IsColimit (colimit.cocone D) := colimit.isColimit D
138	  have hLc := isColimitOfPreserves (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj)) hc
139	  have hF := isColimitOfPreserves
140	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj) ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) hc
141	  haveI hpres : PreservesColimit (D ⋙ PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj))
142	      (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) :=
143	    preservesColimit_of_preserves_colimit_cocone hLc hF
144	  exact preservesColimit_of_iso_diagram (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) e.symm
145	
146	/-- **The underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves epimorphisms** (blueprint
147	`lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`).  A one-line corollary of `toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`:
148	finite-colimit preservation implies preservation of pushouts (`WalkingSpan`), hence of
149	epimorphisms.  This is the instance unlocking the passage from a module epimorphism to local
150	surjectivity in `affine_surj_of_vanishing`. -/
151	theorem toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms.{v', u', w'} {C : Type u'} [Category.{w'} C]
152	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} (R : Sheaf J RingCat.{v'})
153	    [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] :
154	    (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R).PreservesEpimorphisms := by
155	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfShape WalkingSpan (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R) :=
156	    (toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits R).preservesFiniteColimits WalkingSpan
157	  exact preservesEpimorphisms_of_preservesColimitsOfShape _
158	
159	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — standard covers are cofinal -/
160	
161	/-- **Standard covers are cofinal among open covers of a distinguished open** (Stacks 02KG, Tag
162	009L; blueprint `lem:standard_cover_cofinal`).  Given a distinguished open `D(f)` of `Spec R` and an
163	arbitrary open cover `W : α → (Spec R).Opens` of it, there is a finite standard subcover refining
164	it: finitely many `g : Fin n → R` with `D(f) = ⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` and each `D(gᵢ) ≤ W (φ i)`.  This is the
165	refinement step invoked in `affine_surj_of_vanishing`.  Project-local: combines quasi-compactness of
166	`D(f)` with the basic-open basis. -/
167	theorem standard_cover_cofinal {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (f : R) {α : Type u}
168	    (W : α → (Spec R).Opens)
169	    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ ⨆ a, W a) :
170	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → α),
171	      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ∧
172	      ∀ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ W (φ i) := by
173	  classical
174	  set Uf : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f with hUf
175	  have hK : IsCompact (Uf : Set ↥(Spec R)) := PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen f
176	  have hbasis := PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens (R := R)
177	  -- `B r` is the distinguished open `D(r)` typed as an open of `Spec R`.
178	  let B : R → (Spec R).Opens := fun r => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
179	  -- Index type: basic opens contained in `Uf ⊓ W a`, carrying the witness `a`.
180	  let I := {p : R × α // B p.1 ≤ Uf ⊓ W p.2}
181	  let cover : I → Set ↥(Spec R) := fun p => (B p.1.1 : Set ↥(Spec R))
182	  have hopen : ∀ i, IsOpen (cover i) := fun i => (B i.1.1).isOpen
183	  -- The chosen basic opens cover `Uf`.
184	  have hsub : (Uf : Set ↥(Spec R)) ⊆ ⋃ i, cover i := by
185	    intro x hx
186	    have hxUf : x ∈ Uf := hx
187	    obtain ⟨a, hxa⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp (hcov hxUf)
188	    have hxinf : x ∈ ((Uf ⊓ W a : (Spec R).Opens) : Set ↥(Spec R)) := by
189	      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inf]
190	      exact ⟨hxUf, hxa⟩
191	    obtain ⟨v, hvrange, hxv, hvsub⟩ :=
192	      hbasis.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxinf (Uf ⊓ W a).isOpen
193	    obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := hvrange
194	    have hr' : (B r : Set ↥(Spec R)) = v := hr
195	    have hle : B r ≤ Uf ⊓ W a := by
196	      rw [← SetLike.coe_subset_coe, hr']; exact hvsub
197	    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨(r, a), hle⟩, ?_⟩
198	    change x ∈ (B r : Set ↥(Spec R))
199	    rw [hr']; exact hxv
200	  -- Quasi-compactness: extract a finite subcover.
201	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := hK.elim_finite_subcover cover hopen hsub
202	  -- Repackage the finite index set `t : Finset I` as `Fin n`.
203	  let e := t.equivFin
204	  refine ⟨t.card, fun i => (e.symm i).1.1.1, fun i => (e.symm i).1.1.2, ?_, ?_⟩
205	  · apply le_antisymm
206	    · intro x hx
207	      obtain ⟨i, hit, hxi⟩ := Set.mem_iUnion₂.mp (ht hx)
208	      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
209	      refine ⟨e ⟨i, hit⟩, ?_⟩
210	      change x ∈ B (e.symm (e ⟨i, hit⟩)).1.1.1
211	      rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]; exact hxi
212	    · rw [iSup_le_iff]
213	      intro i
214	      exact le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_left
215	  · intro i
216	    exact le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_right
217	
218	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section surjectivity for the affine cover system -/
219	
220	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
221	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
222	/-- **Section surjectivity for the affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG, `surj_of_vanishing` field;
223	Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`). Let `S : 0 → S₁ → S₂ → S₃ → 0` be a short exact sequence of
224	`O_X`-modules on `Spec R` whose left term `S₁` has vanishing positive Čech cohomology over every
225	standard cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` (`hvanish`). Then the section map `S₂(D f) → S₃(D f)` is surjective over
226	every distinguished open `D f`. This discharges the `surj_of_vanishing` field of `affineCoverSystem`.
227	
228	Proof: `S.g` is an epi of `O_X`-modules, so (via `toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms` + the sheaf
229	local-surjectivity criterion) the underlying map of abelian presheaves is locally surjective. A
230	section `t ∈ S₃(D f)` therefore lifts locally on an open cover of `D f`; refine it to a standard
231	cover `D(gᵢ)` (`standard_cover_cofinal`) carrying local lifts, feed the cover, the lifts and the
232	vanishing `Ȟ¹(𝒰, S₁) = 0` to the Čech `Ȟ¹`-surjectivity criterion `ses_cech_h1`, and glue to a
233	global lift. Project-local: the affine instantiation of the `ses_cech_h1` criterion. -/
234	theorem affine_surj_of_vanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
235	    (S : ShortComplex (Spec R).Modules) (hS : S.ShortExact)
236	    (hvanish : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (f' : R),
237	      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f' : (Spec R).Opens)
238	        = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) →
239	      ∀ (q : ℕ), 0 < q →
240	        IsZero (cechCohomology (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
241	          ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁) q))
242	    (f : R) :
243	    Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
244	      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
245	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g)).app
246	          (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) := by
247	  classical
248	  set V₀ : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f with hV₀
249	  set FX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁ with hFX
250	  set GX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₂ with hGX
251	  set HX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₃ with hHX
252	  set fι := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
253	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.f) with hfι
254	  set gπ := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
255	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g) with hgπ
256	  -- Step 1: a module epi becomes a locally surjective map of abelian presheaves.
257	  haveI hgepi : Epi S.g := hS.epi_g
258	  have hepiTS : Epi ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g) :=
259	    @Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf)
260	      (toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) _ _ S.g hgepi
261	  have hls : TopCat.Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective gπ :=
262	    (Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi' AddCommGrpCat.{u}
263	      ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g)).mpr hepiTS
264	  rw [TopCat.Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff] at hls
265	  intro t
266	  -- Step 2: per-point local lifts of `t`.
267	  have hch : ∀ p : ↥V₀, ∃ (W : (Spec R).Opens) (hWle : W ≤ V₀)
268	      (sl : ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))),
269	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (Opposite.op W)) sl
270	        = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWle).op) t ∧ (p : Spec R) ∈ W := by
271	    intro p
272	    obtain ⟨W, hWle, ⟨sl, hsl⟩, hmem⟩ := hls V₀ t p.1 p.2
273	    exact ⟨W, hWle, sl, hsl, hmem⟩
274	  choose W hWle sLift hsLift hmem using hch
275	  have hVcov : V₀ ≤ ⨆ p : ↥V₀, W p := by
276	    intro x hx
277	    exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨⟨x, hx⟩, hmem ⟨x, hx⟩⟩
278	  -- Step 3: refine to a standard cover `D(gᵢ)`.
279	  obtain ⟨n, g, φ, hVeq, hgle⟩ := standard_cover_cofinal f W hVcov
280	  set U : ULift.{u} (Fin n) → (Spec R).Opens := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) with hU
281	  have hUsup : ⨆ i, U i = V₀ := by
282	    rw [hU, hV₀, hVeq]
283	    exact Equiv.ulift.{u}.iSup_comp (g := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i))
284	  -- the restricted local lifts on the standard cover
285	  set sLoc : ∀ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (U i))) :=
286	    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom (GX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op)
287	      (sLift (φ i.down)) with hsLoc
288	  -- the section `t` transported to `⨆ U` (`= V₀`)
289	  have hopV : (Opposite.op (⨆ i, U i) : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (Spec R))ᵒᵖ) = Opposite.op V₀ :=
290	    congrArg Opposite.op hUsup
291	  set s : ToType (HX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨆ i, U i))) :=
292	    ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t with hs
293	  -- assemble the hypotheses of `ses_cech_h1`
294	  have hπι : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (FX.presheaf.obj V)),
295	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0 := by
296	    intro V x
297	    have hz : fι ≫ gπ = 0 := by
298	      rw [hfι, hgπ, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp, S.zero,
299	        Functor.map_zero, Functor.map_zero]
300	    have h := congrArg (fun (ψ : FX.presheaf ⟶ HX.presheaf) => ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app V) x) hz
301	    simp at h ⊢
302	    exact h
303	  have hmono : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ),
304	      Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)) := by
305	    intro V
306	    haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
307	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
308	    haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) := by
309	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
310	    haveI : Mono S.f := hS.mono_f
311	    have hmonoAb : Mono (fι.app V) :=
312	      inferInstanceAs (Mono ((sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).map S.f))
313	    rwa [AddCommGrpCat.mono_iff_injective] at hmonoAb
314	  have hker : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (GX.presheaf.obj V)),
315	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 → ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x := by
316	    intro V x hx
317	    haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
318	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
319	    haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) := by
320	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
321	    have hex : (S.map (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V))).Exact :=
322	      ShortComplex.Exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel hS.exact
323	        (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) hS.mono_f inferInstance
324	    rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hex

366	/-- **The affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG, `def:affine_cover_system`). For `X = Spec R`, the
367	basis `B` is the distinguished opens `D f` (`f : R`) and the admissible coverings `Cov` are the
368	standard finite covers `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` (indexed by `ULift (Fin n)`, so as to land in `Type u`). The
369	three nontrivial fields are discharged by:
370	- `faces_mem` ← `affine_faces_mem` (finite intersections of distinguished opens are distinguished);
371	- `surj_of_vanishing` ← `affine_surj_of_vanishing` (the `ses_cech_h1` section-surjectivity criterion);
372	- `injective_acyclic` ← `injective_cech_acyclicFam` (cover-agnostic injective Čech-acyclicity, applied
373	  directly to the distinguished opens of each standard cover — no `Spec R_f` restriction detour).
374	Project-local: the affine instantiation of `BasisCovSystem` consumed by `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`. -/
375	noncomputable def affineCoverSystem (R : CommRingCat.{u}) : BasisCovSystem (Spec R) where
376	  B := Set.range (fun f : R => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens))
377	  Cov := { c : CovDatum (Spec R) | ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (f : R),
378	    c = ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩ ∧
379	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)

397	
398	/-- **A standard cover of `D(f)` spans the unit ideal of `R_f`.** If the distinguished opens
399	`D(gᵢ)` cover `D(f)` in `Spec R`, then the images `gᵢ ↦ R_f` of the cover family span the whole
400	ring `R_f = Localization.Away f`: pulled back along `Spec R_f ≅ D(f) ↪ Spec R` the union of the
401	`D(gᵢ)` becomes `D(f)` itself, which is all of `Spec R_f` since `f` is a unit there. Project-local:
402	the spanning hypothesis required to apply the standard-cover tilde {\v C}ech vanishing
403	`sectionCech_affine_vanishing` over `R_f` in the quasi-coherent seed's change-of-base leaf. -/

405	    (g : ι → R) (f : R)
406	    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
407	      = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i)) :
408	    Ideal.span (Set.range (fun i => algebraMap R (Localization.Away f) (g i))) = ⊤ := by

410	  simp only [← PrimeSpectrum.comap_basicOpen]
411	  rw [← map_iSup, ← hcov, PrimeSpectrum.comap_basicOpen, eq_top_iff]
412	  rintro p -
413	  rw [PrimeSpectrum.mem_basicOpen]
414	  exact fun hmem => p.isPrime.ne_top
415	    (Ideal.eq_top_of_isUnit_mem _ hmem (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit f))

417	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech cohomology transports along isomorphisms -/
418	
419	/-- **{\v C}ech cohomology transports along an isomorphism of coefficient presheaves.** An
420	isomorphism `e : F ≅ G` of presheaves of `𝒪_X`-modules induces an isomorphism of section {\v C}ech
421	complexes (`sectionCechComplexFunctor`), hence of their degree-`p` homologies; so vanishing of
422	`Ȟᵖ(𝒰, F)` transfers to `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, G)`. Project-local: the naturality used to reduce the
423	quasi-coherent {\v C}ech-vanishing seed to the tilde case via `F ≅ ~(ΓF)`. -/
424	theorem cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
425	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (e : F ≅ G) (p : ℕ)
426	    (h : IsZero (cechCohomology U F p)) : IsZero (cechCohomology U G p) :=
427	  h.of_iso ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor Ab.{u} (ComplexShape.up ℕ) p).mapIso
428	    ((sectionCechComplexFunctor U).mapIso e)).symm

431	
432	/-- **Reduction of the quasi-coherent {\v C}ech-vanishing seed to the tilde case.**  For a
433	quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`, the affine structure theorem `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`
434	gives `F ≅ ~M` with `M = Γ(Spec R, F)`; transporting along this isomorphism
435	(`cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso`) reduces the seed `HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystem R) F`
436	to the tilde-case standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing supplied in `htilde`.  Project-local: isolates
437	the single remaining geometric obligation (positive-degree {\v C}ech vanishing of `~M` over a
438	standard cover of a distinguished open `D(f)`), which is the change-of-base-to-`R_f` leaf. -/
439	theorem affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
440	    (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
441	    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (f : R),
442	        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
443	          = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) →
444	        ∀ p, 0 < p →
445	          IsZero (cechCohomology
446	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
447	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
448	              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p)) :
449	    HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystem R) F := by
450	  intro c hc p hp
451	  obtain ⟨n, g, f, rfl, hcov⟩ := hc
452	  refine cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso _
453	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).mapIso
454	      (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).symm) p ?_
455	  exact htilde n g f hcov p hp

562	      ∀ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ W (φ i) := by
563	  classical
564	  have hK : IsCompact (V : Set ↥(Spec R)) := hV.isCompact
565	  have hbasis := PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens (R := R)
566	  -- `B r` is the distinguished open `D(r)` typed as an open of `Spec R`.

568	  -- Index type: basic opens contained in `V ⊓ W a`, carrying the witness `a`.

570	  let cover : I → Set ↥(Spec R) := fun p => (B p.1.1 : Set ↥(Spec R))
571	  have hopen : ∀ i, IsOpen (cover i) := fun i => (B i.1.1).isOpen
572	  -- The chosen basic opens cover `V`.

574	    intro x hx
575	    have hxV : x ∈ V := hx
576	    obtain ⟨a, hxa⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp (hcov hxV)
577	    have hxinf : x ∈ ((V ⊓ W a : (Spec R).Opens) : Set ↥(Spec R)) := by
578	      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inf]

581	      hbasis.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxinf (V ⊓ W a).isOpen

585	      rw [← SetLike.coe_subset_coe, hr']; exact hvsub
586	    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨(r, a), hle⟩, ?_⟩

599	      rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]; exact hxi

608	/-- **Section surjectivity for the enlarged affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG, `surj_of_vanishing`
609	field, general-affine-open form), generalising `affine_surj_of_vanishing` from a distinguished `D f`
610	to an *arbitrary affine open* `V₀`.  Let `S : 0 → S₁ → S₂ → S₃ → 0` be short exact with `S₁` having

616	(`V₀ = ⨆ D(gᵢ)`) fed to `hvanish`. Project-local: discharges the `surj_of_vanishing` field of

618	theorem affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
619	    (S : ShortComplex (Spec R).Modules) (hS : S.ShortExact)
620	    (hvanish : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),

622	          (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) →
623	      ∀ (q : ℕ), 0 < q →
624	        IsZero (cechCohomology (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
625	          ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁) q))
626	    (V₀ : (Spec R).Opens) (hV₀ : IsAffineOpen V₀) :
627	    Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
628	      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
629	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g)).app
630	          (Opposite.op V₀))) := by
631	  classical
632	  set FX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁ with hFX
633	  set GX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₂ with hGX
634	  set HX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₃ with hHX
635	  set fι := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
636	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.f) with hfι
637	  set gπ := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
638	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g) with hgπ
639	  -- Step 1: a module epi becomes a locally surjective map of abelian presheaves.
640	  haveI hgepi : Epi S.g := hS.epi_g
641	  have hepiTS : Epi ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g) :=
642	    @Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf)
643	      (toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) _ _ S.g hgepi
644	  have hls : TopCat.Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective gπ :=
645	    (Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi' AddCommGrpCat.{u}
646	      ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g)).mpr hepiTS
647	  rw [TopCat.Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff] at hls
648	  intro t
649	  -- Step 2: per-point local lifts of `t`.
650	  have hch : ∀ p : ↥V₀, ∃ (W : (Spec R).Opens) (hWle : W ≤ V₀)
651	      (sl : ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))),
652	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (Opposite.op W)) sl
653	        = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWle).op) t ∧ (p : Spec R) ∈ W := by
654	    intro p
655	    obtain ⟨W, hWle, ⟨sl, hsl⟩, hmem⟩ := hls V₀ t p.1 p.2
656	    exact ⟨W, hWle, sl, hsl, hmem⟩
657	  choose W hWle sLift hsLift hmem using hch
658	  have hVcov : V₀ ≤ ⨆ p : ↥V₀, W p := by
659	    intro x hx
660	    exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨⟨x, hx⟩, hmem ⟨x, hx⟩⟩
661	  -- Step 3: refine to a standard cover `D(gᵢ)` of the affine open `V₀`.
662	  obtain ⟨n, g, φ, hVeq, hgle⟩ := standard_cover_cofinal_affine V₀ hV₀ W hVcov
663	  set U : ULift.{u} (Fin n) → (Spec R).Opens := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) with hU
664	  have hUsup : ⨆ i, U i = V₀ := by
665	    rw [hU, hVeq]
666	    exact Equiv.ulift.{u}.iSup_comp (g := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i))
667	  -- the cover's union is affine (it equals `V₀`), so it is an admissible covering.
668	  have haff : IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
669	      (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) := hUsup ▸ hV₀
670	  -- the restricted local lifts on the standard cover
671	  set sLoc : ∀ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (U i))) :=
672	    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom (GX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op)
673	      (sLift (φ i.down)) with hsLoc
674	  -- the section `t` transported to `⨆ U` (`= V₀`)
675	  have hopV : (Opposite.op (⨆ i, U i) : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (Spec R))ᵒᵖ) = Opposite.op V₀ :=
676	    congrArg Opposite.op hUsup
677	  set s : ToType (HX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨆ i, U i))) :=
678	    ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t with hs
679	  -- assemble the hypotheses of `ses_cech_h1`
680	  have hπι : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (FX.presheaf.obj V)),
681	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0 := by
682	    intro V x
683	    have hz : fι ≫ gπ = 0 := by
684	      rw [hfι, hgπ, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp, S.zero,
685	        Functor.map_zero, Functor.map_zero]
686	    have h := congrArg (fun (ψ : FX.presheaf ⟶ HX.presheaf) => ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app V) x) hz
687	    simp at h ⊢
688	    exact h
689	  have hmono : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ),
690	      Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)) := by
691	    intro V
692	    haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
693	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
694	    haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) := by
695	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
696	    haveI : Mono S.f := hS.mono_f
697	    have hmonoAb : Mono (fι.app V) :=
698	      inferInstanceAs (Mono ((sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).map S.f))
699	    rwa [AddCommGrpCat.mono_iff_injective] at hmonoAb
700	  have hker : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (GX.presheaf.obj V)),
701	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 → ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x := by
702	    intro V x hx
703	    haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
704	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
705	    haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) := by
706	      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
707	    have hex : (S.map (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V))).Exact :=
708	      ShortComplex.Exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel hS.exact
709	        (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) hS.mono_f inferInstance
710	    rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hex
711	    exact (hex x).mp hx
712	  -- the vanishing input, packaged as homology of the section Čech complex
713	  have hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U FX).homology 1) := hvanish n g haff 1 one_pos
714	  -- the local-lift compatibility `gπ(sLocᵢ) = s |_ Uᵢ`
715	  have hlift : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (Opposite.op (U i))) (sLoc i)
716	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op) s := by
717	    intro i
718	    simp only [hsLoc, hs]
719	    erw [gπ.naturality_apply (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op (sLift (φ i.down)), hsLift (φ i.down)]
720	    show ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op)
721	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hWle (φ i.down))).op) t)
722	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op)
723	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t)
724	    have hmaps : HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hWle (φ i.down))).op
725	          ≫ HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op
726	        = HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)
727	          ≫ HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op := by
728	      rw [← HX.presheaf.map_comp, ← HX.presheaf.map_comp]
729	      congr 1
730	    simp only [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
731	    rw [← hmaps]
732	  -- apply the Čech surjectivity criterion
733	  obtain ⟨glob, hglob⟩ := ses_cech_h1 U FX GX HX fι gπ S.X₂.isSheaf S.X₃.isSheaf
734	    hπι hmono hker s hH1 sLoc hlift
735	  -- transport the global lift back to `V₀`
736	  refine ⟨ConcreteCategory.hom (GX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV)) glob, ?_⟩
737	  erw [gπ.naturality_apply (eqToHom hopV) glob, hglob]
738	  show ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV))
739	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t) = t
740	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← HX.presheaf.map_comp]
741	  simp only [eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
742	  rfl
743	
744	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the enlarged affine cover system -/
745	
746	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
747	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
748	-- The structure literal forces three nontrivial field elaborations (`faces_mem`,
749	-- `surj_of_vanishing`, `injective_acyclic`), each unfolding large presheaf data.
750	/-- **The enlarged affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG), with basis `B` the *all affine opens* of
751	`Spec R` (rather than only the distinguished `D f` of `affineCoverSystem`) and admissible coverings
752	`Cov` the standard finite covers `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` whose union `⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` is affine.  The three fields:
753	- `faces_mem` ← faces `⨅ₖ D(g_{σk}) = D(∏ g)` are distinguished, hence affine
754	  (`isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen`);
755	- `surj_of_vanishing` ← `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` (general-affine-open surjectivity);
756	- `injective_acyclic` ← `injective_cech_acyclicFam` (cover-agnostic injective Čech-acyclicity).
757	Project-local: the enlarged instantiation of `BasisCovSystem` consumed by
758	`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` to obtain general-affine-open Serre vanishing. -/
759	noncomputable def affineCoverSystemGeneral (R : CommRingCat.{u}) : BasisCovSystem (Spec R) where
760	  B := { U : (Spec R).Opens | IsAffineOpen U }
761	  Cov := { c : CovDatum (Spec R) | ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
762	    c = ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩ ∧
763	    IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
764	      (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) }
765	  faces_mem := by
766	    rintro c ⟨n, g, rfl, hcov⟩ p σ
767	    have h := basicOpen_sprod (p + 1) (fun j : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g j.down) σ
768	    exact h ▸ isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen
769	      (∏ k, (fun j : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g j.down) (σ k))
770	  surj_of_vanishing := by
771	    rintro S hSE hvanish V hV
772	    refine affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine S hSE (fun n g haff q hq => ?_) V hV
773	    exact hvanish ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩
774	      ⟨n, g, rfl, haff⟩ q hq
775	  injective_acyclic := by
776	    rintro I hI c ⟨n, g, rfl, hcov⟩ q hq
777	    haveI : Injective I := hI
778	    have hfam := injective_cech_acyclicFam
779	      (U := fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) I q hq
780	    exact hfam
781	
782	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — general-affine-open Serre vanishing (reduced to the seed)
783	
784	The **residual** of the enlarged route is the positive-degree quasi-coherent Čech-vanishing *seed*
785	`HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F`: vanishing of `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, F)` for `p > 0` over
786	every standard cover `𝒰 : i ↦ D(gᵢ)` *whose union `⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` is affine* (not just a distinguished
787	`D(f)`). Unlike the distinguished case — discharged by
788	`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway` via change of base to a single
789	`Rf = Localization.Away f` — a general affine open `V = ⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` has no single `f` with `V = D(f)`,
790	so the seed needs a change of base to `Γ(V, 𝒪)` (equivalently, the identification of the section
791	Čech complex over `{D(gᵢ) ⊆ Spec R}` with the standard-cover complex of `~(Γ(V,F))` over
792	`Spec Γ(V)` under `V ≅ Spec Γ(V)`). That change-of-scheme identification is the lone remaining
793	geometric leaf; it is isolated here as the explicit hypothesis `hseed`. -/
794	
795	/-- **Reduction of the general-affine-open quasi-coherent seed to the tilde case.**  For a
796	quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`, transporting along `F ≅ ~M` (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`,
797	`M = Γ(Spec R, F)`) reduces the seed `HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F` to the
798	tilde-case positive-degree Čech vanishing `htilde`: vanishing of `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, ~M)` over every standard
799	cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` whose union is affine.  Verbatim analogue of
800	`affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing` for the enlarged cover system; isolates the single
801	geometric residual (the change-of-base-to-`Γ(V)` tilde-section identification).  Project-local: the
802	cleanest statement of the general-affine-open seed residual. -/
803	theorem affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
804	    (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
805	    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
806	        IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
807	            (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) →
808	        ∀ p, 0 < p →
809	          IsZero (cechCohomology
810	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
811	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
812	              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p)) :
813	    HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F := by
814	  intro c hc p hp
815	  obtain ⟨n, g, rfl, hcov⟩ := hc
816	  refine cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso _
817	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).mapIso
818	      (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).symm) p ?_
819	  exact htilde n g hcov p hp
820	
821	/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, reduced to the Čech-vanishing seed** (Stacks 02KG,
822	generalised from `D(f)` to an arbitrary affine open).  For a quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`
823	and **any** affine open `V` of `Spec R`, the absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(V, F) = Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F)`
824	vanishes for `p > 0`, modulo the general-affine-open Čech-vanishing seed `hseed`.  Instantiates the
825	basis-comparison criterion `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` at the enlarged cover system
826	`affineCoverSystemGeneral R` (whose basis is all affine opens).  Project-local: the Need #2 target
827	of `analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md`, isolating the single seed residual. -/
828	theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
829	    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
830	    (hseed : HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F)
831	    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
832	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
833	  cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) hseed V hV p hp e
834	
835	/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, reduced to the tilde Čech-vanishing leaf**
836	(Stacks 02KG, generalised).  Composes `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing` (seed
837	from tilde vanishing) with `affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed`: for quasi-coherent `F` and any
838	affine open `V`, `Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F) = 0` for `p > 0`, modulo the tilde-section change-of-base
839	leaf `htilde`.  Project-local: the fully-reduced Need #2 top, leaving exactly the change-of-scheme
840	tilde residual. -/
841	theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
842	    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
843	    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
844	        IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
845	            (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) →
846	        ∀ p, 0 < p →
847	          IsZero (cechCohomology
848	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
849	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
850	              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p))
851	    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
852	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
853	  affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed F
854	    (affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing F htilde) V hV p hp e
855	
856	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the unconditional general-affine 02KG tops (Need #2) -/
857	
858	/-- **The general-affine standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing residual, discharged unconditionally.**
859	The general-affine analogue of `affine_tildeVanishing`: for a quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`
860	with global module `M = Γ(Spec R, F)`, the positive-degree section {\v C}ech cohomology of the tilde
861	sheaf `~M` over any standard cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` *whose union is an affine open* vanishes. Proved by
862	the now-built change-of-base-to-`Γ(V)` theorem `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen` (route B1,
863	base change to `S = Γ(V)`), with `s := g ∘ down`, replacing the single-`R_f` change of base of
864	`affine_tildeVanishing`. Project-local: bundles the residual leaf in the precise shape the general
865	`_of_tildeVanishing` forms consume. -/
866	private theorem affine_tildeVanishing_general {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (F : (Spec R).Modules)
867	    (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R)
868	    (haff : IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
869	      (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)))
870	    (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p) :
871	    IsZero (cechCohomology
872	      (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
873	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
874	        (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p) :=
875	  sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F)
876	    (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g i.down) haff p hp
877	
878	/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, unconditional** (Stacks 02KG, generalised from
879	`D(f)` to an arbitrary affine open; closes Need #2). For a quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`
880	and **any** affine open `V` of `Spec R`, the absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(V, F) = Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F)`
881	vanishes for `p > 0`. Obtained by discharging the tilde-{\v C}ech-vanishing leaf `htilde` of
882	`affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing` with the now-proved residual
883	`affine_tildeVanishing_general` (in turn `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen`). Project-local:
884	the general-affine 02KG top, discharging the Need #2 general-affine cone end-to-end. -/
885	theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_open {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
886	    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
887	    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
888	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
889	  affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing F
890	    (affine_tildeVanishing_general F) V hV p hp e

lemma Spec_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat} (f : R) :
    IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) :=
  basicOpen_eq_of_affine f ▸ (isAffineOpen_top (Spec <| .of R)).basicOpen _

instance [IsAffine X] (r : Γ(X, ⊤)) : IsAffine (X.basicOpen r) :=
  (isAffineOpen_top X).basicOpen _

include hU in
theorem ι_basicOpen_preimage (r : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
    IsAffineOpen ((X.basicOpen r).ι ⁻¹ᵁ U) := by
  apply (X.basicOpen r).ι.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion.mp
  rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι, inf_comm,
    ← Scheme.basicOpen_res _ _ (homOfLE le_top).op]
  exact hU.basicOpen _

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
include hU in
theorem exists_basicOpen_le {V : X.Opens} (x : V) (h : ↑x ∈ U) :
    ∃ f : Γ(X, U), X.basicOpen f ≤ V ∧ ↑x ∈ X.basicOpen f := by
  have : IsAffine _ := hU
  obtain ⟨_, ⟨_, ⟨r, rfl⟩, rfl⟩, h₁, h₂ : _ ≤ U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V⟩ :=
    (isBasis_basicOpen U).exists_subset_of_mem_open (x.2 : (⟨x, h⟩ : U) ∈ _) (U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V).isOpen
  replace h₁ : x.1 ∈ X.basicOpen r := by simpa [U.mem_basicOpen_toScheme] using! h₁
  replace h₂ : X.basicOpen r ≤ V := by
    simpa [Scheme.image_basicOpen] using! (U.ι.image_mono h₂).trans (U.ι.image_preimage_le _)
  exact ⟨U.topIso.hom.hom r, by simp [Scheme.Opens.toScheme_presheaf_obj, h₁, h₂]⟩

noncomputable
instance {R : CommRingCat} {U} : Algebra R Γ(Spec R, U) :=
  inferInstanceAs (Algebra R ((Spec.structureSheaf R).presheaf.obj _))

@[simp]
lemma algebraMap_Spec_obj {R : CommRingCat} {U} : algebraMap R Γ(Spec R, U) =
    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv ≫ (Spec R).presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom := rfl

instance {R : CommRingCat} {f : R} :
    IsLocalization.Away f Γ(Spec R, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsLocalization.Away f
    ((Spec.structureSheaf R).obj.obj (op <| PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))

/-- Given an affine open U and some `f : U`,
this is the canonical map `Γ(𝒪ₓ, D(f)) ⟶ Γ(Spec 𝒪ₓ(U), D(f))`
This is an isomorphism, as witnessed by an `IsIso` instance. -/
def basicOpenSectionsToAffine :
    Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) ⟶ Γ(Spec Γ(X, U), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) :=
  hU.fromSpec.app (X.basicOpen f) ≫
    (Spec Γ(X, U)).presheaf.map (eqToHom (hU.fromSpec_preimage_basicOpen f).symm).op

instance basicOpenSectionsToAffine_isIso :
    IsIso (basicOpenSectionsToAffine hU f) :=
  (hU.fromSpec.isIso_app _ (hU.opensRange_fromSpec.symm ▸ X.basicOpen_le f)).comp_isIso'
    inferInstance

include hU in
theorem isLocalization_basicOpen :
    IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) := by

1390	end SectionCechModule
1391	
1392	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections as away localisations (L1, step (b))
1393	
1394	The categorical→module bridge needs the section-identification of
1395	`def:qcoh_sections_localized`: over a basic open `D(g)` the sections of a
1396	quasi-coherent sheaf are the away localisation `M_g`, and the restriction maps
1397	between basic opens are the canonical localisation comparison maps.  For the
1398	standard sheaf `tilde M` this is *verbatim* from Mathlib's `Tilde` development
1399	(`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen` carries `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g)` and the
1400	restriction compatibility is `tilde.toOpen_res`); the only project-local content is
1401	(i) the multi-index intersection `⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}) = D(s_σ)` identification, which lets
1402	the degree-`p` section group over the `(p+1)`-fold intersection be read as the
1403	localisation `M_{s_σ}` (this is what `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes
1404	degreewise), and (ii) the identification of the abstract presheaf restriction with
1405	`AwayComparison.comparison`, the differential brick.
1406	
1407	For an *arbitrary* quasi-coherent `F` the remaining input is the affine equivalence
1408	`F ≅ tilde(ΓF)` (Stacks 01I8); see `def:qcoh_sections_localized`.  The tilde case
1409	below is the gap-free part that lands the named target. -/
1410	
1411	/-- Splitting an indexed infimum over `Fin (n+1)` into the `0`-th term and the
1412	infimum over the tail.  Lattice-combinatorial helper behind `basicOpen_sprod`. -/
1413	private lemma iInf_fin_succ {α : Type*} [CompleteLattice α] (n : ℕ) (f : Fin (n + 1) → α) :
1414	    (⨅ i, f i) = f 0 ⊓ ⨅ i : Fin n, f i.succ := by
1415	  apply le_antisymm
1416	  · exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iInf fun i => iInf_le _ i.succ)
1417	  · refine le_iInf fun i => ?_
1418	    refine Fin.cases inf_le_left (fun j => le_trans inf_le_right (iInf_le _ j)) i
1419	
1420	/-- **Multi-index basic-open intersection** (geometric input to step (c)): the
1421	`(p+1)`-fold intersection of the basic opens `D(s_{σ k})` of a {\v C}ech multi-index
1422	`σ : Fin n → ι` is the basic open `D(s_σ)` of the product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}`.  This
1423	identifies the section group `F(⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}))` of `sectionCechCosimplicial` with the
1424	away localisation `M_{s_σ}` of `SectionCechModule.dCoeff`. -/
1425	lemma basicOpen_sprod {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} (n : ℕ) (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
1426	    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
1427	      = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (∏ k, s (σ k)) := by
1428	  induction n with
1429	  | zero =>
1430	      rw [show (∏ k, s (σ k)) = 1 from Finset.prod_of_isEmpty _]
1431	      simp only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one]
1432	      exact iInf_of_empty _
1433	  | succ m ih =>
1434	      rw [Fin.prod_univ_succ, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul, ← ih (fun i => σ i.succ),
1435	        iInf_fin_succ]
1436	      rfl
1437	
1438	/-- **Quasi-coherent sections over a {\v C}ech intersection are an away localisation**
1439	(`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, tilde case — step (b) of the L1 bridge).  For the
1440	standard sheaf `tilde M` of an `R`-module `M`, the section-restriction map from `M`
1441	to the sections over the `(p+1)`-fold basic-open intersection
1442	`⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}) = D(s_σ)` exhibits that section group as the away localisation
1443	`M_{s_σ}` (`IsLocalizedModule` for `Submonoid.powers (∏ₖ s_{σ k})`).  Combines the
1444	Mathlib instance `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g) (tilde.toOpen M (D g)).hom` with the
1445	intersection identification `basicOpen_sprod`.  This is the degreewise section
1446	identification that `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes; the restriction
1447	compatibility (item (5) of the blueprint definition) is `tilde.toOpen_res` together
1448	with `qcohRestriction_eq_comparison` below. -/
1449	lemma qcohSectionsAwayLocalized {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u}
1450	    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {n : ℕ} (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
1451	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1452	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom := by
1453	  rw [basicOpen_sprod]
1454	  infer_instance
1455	
1456	/-- **Restriction = localisation comparison** (`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, item (5);
1457	the differential brick of step (c)).  For the standard sheaf `tilde M`, the presheaf
1458	restriction map between basic-open section groups `M_a → M_b` (along an inclusion
1459	`D(b) ⊆ D(a)`) is, as an `R`-linear map, the canonical away-localisation comparison
1460	`AwayComparison.comparison` — provided `a` acts invertibly on `M_b` (`Inverts a M_b`,
1461	which holds whenever `a ∣ b`, the {\v C}ech-face case).  Proved by the universal
1462	property `AwayComparison.comparison_unique`: both the restriction and the comparison
1463	are `R`-linear maps that recover `tilde.toOpen M (D b)` after precomposition with
1464	`tilde.toOpen M (D a)` (the restriction does so by `tilde.toOpen_res`).  Summed over
1465	the alternating signs, this identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with the
1466	module differential `SectionCechModule.dDiff`. -/
1467	lemma qcohRestriction_eq_comparison {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {a b : R}
1468	    (i : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b : (Spec R).Opens) ⟶ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1469	    (hb : AwayComparison.Inverts a
1470	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
1471	        (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)))) :
1472	    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op).hom
1473	      = AwayComparison.comparison (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1474	          (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb := by
1475	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
1476	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom := inferInstance
1477	  refine (AwayComparison.comparison_unique
1478	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1479	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb _ ?_).symm
1480	  have h := AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen_res M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1481	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b) i
1482	  exact congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom h

1485	
1486	The categorical→module bridge of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact`: the
1487	`Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (of `PresheafCech.lean`)
1488	has its degree-`p` object a *categorical product* `∏ᶜ_σ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` in `Ab`, and
1489	its differential the alternating sum of the {\v C}ech coface restrictions.  These
1490	lemmas (c1)–(c3) move that abstract complex to the concrete localised-module complex
1491	`SectionCechModule.dDiff` (whose positive-degree exactness `dDiff_exact` is step (a)),
1492	and read off homology vanishing. -/
1493	
1494	section SectionCechBridge
1495	
1496	open CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicTopology
1497	
1498	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1499	
1500	/-- **(c1) Element-level product equivalence** (`lem:section_cech_product_equiv`):
1501	the underlying type of the degree-`p` object `∏ᶜ_σ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of the section
1502	{\v C}ech cosimplicial object is the dependent product `∏_σ ToType (F(⨅ₖ U (σ k)))`.
1503	Supplied by `CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv` (the forgetful functor of
1504	`Ab` preserves discrete products). -/
1505	noncomputable def sectionCechProductEquiv {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1506	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) :
1507	    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≃
1508	      (∀ σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι, ToType (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) :=
1509	  Concrete.productEquiv
1510	    (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
1511	
1512	/-- Coordinate projection of `sectionCechProductEquiv`: the `σ`-component is the
1513	underlying group map of the categorical projection `Pi.π … σ`.  A definitional
1514	restatement of `Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply`, named so that downstream proofs can
1515	`rw` it without fighting the `Concrete.productEquiv` coercion. -/
1516	lemma sectionCechProductEquiv_apply {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1517	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ)
1518	    (y : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
1519	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
1520	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F p y σ
1521	      = ConcreteCategory.hom
1522	          (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ) y :=
1523	  Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply _ y σ
1524	
1525	/-- **Homology-to-exactness reduction** (the `Ab`-side of `lem:section_cech_ab_exact`):
1526	the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the section {\v C}ech complex vanishes once the
1527	underlying group homomorphisms of the two consecutive coface differentials
1528	`objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence.  Pure homological algebra: combines
1529	`exactAt_iff_isZero_homology`, `exactAt_iff'`, and the abelian-group exactness
1530	criterion `ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact`. -/
1531	lemma sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact {ι : Type u}
1532	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
1533	    (h : Function.Exact
1534	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
1535	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
1536	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1)))) :
1537	    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1)) := by
1538	  rw [← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
1539	      (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),

text:
# Scouting Report: Porting the Stacks-01EO Čech-Vanishing Seed to a General Scheme's Affine Opens

## 1. The algebraic heart — pure ring/module-level statement

**Home file: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean`** (NOT `TildeExactness.lean`/`AffineSerreVanishing.lean`/`QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean`/`QcohTildeSections.lean` — those are downstream consumers/identification glue). The reusable algebraic core lives in `namespace SectionCechModule` (opens `CechLocalized AwayComparison`), variables `{R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} (s : ι → R) (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]`.

**Terms** (pure `R`-module data, no scheme/sheaf in sight):
- `CechAcyclic.lean:590` `CechLocalized.sprod {m} (σ : Fin m → ι) : R := ∏ k, s (σ k)` — the localizing element for a Čech multi-index.
- `CechAcyclic.lean:924` `abbrev SectionCechModule.dCoeff {m} (σ : Fin m → ι) : Type u := LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M` — degree-`m` coefficient at multi-index `σ`.
- `CechAcyclic.lean:939` `noncomputable def dDiff (m : ℕ) : (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) →ₗ[R] (∀ σ : Fin (m+1) → ι, dCoeff s M σ)` — the alternating-sum-of-cofaces differential (`LinearMap.pi` of alternating sums of the away-localisation comparison maps `dCoface`).
- `CechAcyclic.lean:945` `dDiff_apply` unfolds it as `∑ j, (-1)^j • dCoface s M m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove))`.

**The three exactness theorems (module-level, no scheme):**

```
-- CechAcyclic.lean:1124
lemma SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact [Finite ι] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (m : ℕ) :
    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2))
```
This is *the* algebraic core: for a spanning family `s : ι → R`, the complex `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` (over `σ : Fin(m+1)→ι`) is exact in positive degrees, proved by `exact_of_isLocalized_span` + the localised combinatorial core `CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact` (localise at each spanning `s r`, reduce to `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`, `CechAcyclic.lean:755`).

```
-- CechAcyclic.lean:1165
lemma SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact_of_localizationAway [Finite ι] (f : R)
    (hmem : ∀ i, ∃ k, f ∣ s i ^ k)
    (hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range (fun i => algebraMap R (Localization.Away f) (s i))) = ⊤)
    (m : ℕ) :
    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2))
```
Route B (change of ring to `Localization.Away f`): `s` need only span the unit ideal after localising at `f` (each `sᵢ ∈ √f`); instantiates `dDiff_exact` over `R_f` and ladder-transports along `AwayComparison.isLocalizedModule_comp_away` (`CechAcyclic.lean:781`).

```
-- CechAcyclic.lean:1290  ← THE ONE TO REUSE for a general affine open
lemma SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact_of_affineCover [Finite ι]
    (S : Type u) [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    (hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range (fun i => algebraMap R S (s i))) = ⊤)
    (hloc : ∀ {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι),
      IsLocalization (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ))
        (Localization (Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R S (sprod s σ)))))
    (m : ℕ) :
    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2))
```
**This is exactly the level of generality the curve-side seed needs.** It is a statement about an abstract ring `R`, an `R`-module `M`, a family `s : ι → R`, and *any* `R`-algebra `S` (in practice `S := Γ(V, 𝒪)` for an affine open `V`) such that (a) the images `s i ↦ S` span the unit ideal of `S`, and (b) for every Čech multi-index `σ`, the `S`-localisation of `M ⊗_R S` at `sprod s σ`'s image is *simultaneously* an `R`-localisation of `M` at `powers (sprod s σ)` (`hloc`, encoding `D(s_σ) ⊆ V`). Route B1: instantiates `dDiff_exact` over `MS := TensorProduct R S M` with base change `bc := TensorProduct.mk R S M 1`, using `isLocalizedModule_baseChange_away` (`CechAcyclic.lean:883`, itself pure base-change algebra: `IsBaseChange.comp` + `isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange`).

**Geometric wrappers** (`R := Γ(Spec R)`-level, still Spec-specific but consuming the above verbatim):
```
-- CechAcyclic.lean:1940
theorem sectionCech_homology_exact {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
    {ι : Type u} [Finite ι] (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)
    (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M))).homology p)

-- CechAcyclic.lean:1973
theorem sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway ... (f : R)
    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
    (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) : IsZero (... .homology p)

-- CechAcyclic.lean:2014
theorem sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineCover ...
    (S : Type u) [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] (hspan) (hloc) (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
    IsZero (... .homology p)

-- CechAcyclic.lean:2073  ← the FULL Spec-side analogue of what you need on X
theorem sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
    {ι : Type u} [Finite ι] (s : ι → R)
    (hV : IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)))
    (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M))).homology p)
```
`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen` (`CechAcyclic.lean:2073–2124`) is *exactly* the pattern to replicate on a general `X`: it sets `S := Γ(V, 𝒪_{Spec R})` where `V = ⨆ᵢ D(sᵢ)`, discharges `hspan` from `hV.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff` and `hloc` per-σ from `hV.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` transported along an `AlgEquiv`.

**Bottom line for reuse on `X`:** you do *not* need to re-derive any Čech combinatorics. `SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact_of_affineCover` is 100% ring/module-theoretic and scheme-independent; the only new work is a geometric wrapper analogous to `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen` that (i) identifies `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)` (`f ∈ Γ(X,U)`, `U` affine ⊆ `X`) as an `R`-localisation of `M := Γ(U, 𝒪_X)` via `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`/`isLocalization_basicOpen`, and (ii) bridges to the *shape* of Čech complex you actually need (see §2/§3 — this is where care is needed because there are two non-defeq Čech complex flavours in the project).

---

## 2. The two Čech complex shapes — NOT literally the same terms

### (a) `sectionCechComplex`/`cechCohomology` — Ab-valued, `X.PresheafOfModules`

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/PresheafCech.lean:303` `sectionCechCosimplicial {ι} (U : ι → Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u}` with
  `obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len+1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))` — a *literal* order-theoretic infimum `⨅ k, U (σ k)` inside `F.presheaf.obj`.
- `PresheafCech.lean:337` `sectionCechComplex U F := (alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ`.
- `CechToCohomology.lean:99` `cechCohomology U F p := (sectionCechComplex U F).homology p : Ab.{u}`.

### (b) `Scheme.cechCochain`/`Scheme.cechCohomology` — `ModuleCat k`-valued, via Mathlib's `cechComplexFunctor`

- `StructureSheafModuleK/Carriers.lean:562` `Scheme.cechCochain (C) (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (𝒰 : ι → Opens C.left.toTopCat) := (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F) : CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ`.
- `:573` `Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n := (Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰).homology n : ModuleCat.{u} k`.
- `:528`/`:541` the original (iter-012) structure-sheaf-specific `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`/`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` with `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`; `:586–599` `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq`/`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` are `rfl`-provable definitional-unfolding bridges (they are just the `F := toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation, not a comparison to flavour (a)).
- `:612` `class Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰` — `Subsingleton (Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n)` for `n > 0` — the **target predicate** your seed presumably needs to instantiate.

### Mathlib's `cechComplexFunctor` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`, read in full)

```
-- FormalCoproducts/Basic.lean, FormalCoproducts/Cech.lean, Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean
cechComplexFunctor U : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ
  := FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech
  := cosimplicialObjectFunctor E ⋙ alternatingCofaceMapComplex A     -- E = (mk _ U).cech
```
Unwinding `evalOp`/`power`/`cech` (all in `FormalCoproducts/Basic.lean`, `FormalCoproducts/Cech.lean`): the degree-`n` term is
```
∏ᶜ (σ : Fin (n+1) → ι), P.obj (op (∏ᶜ k, U (σ k)))
```
where **both** `∏ᶜ` are *categorical* products (via `HasFiniteProducts C`), **not** literal `⨅`/`Pi.lift` over an order-theoretic meet the way `sectionCechCosimplicial` is. For `C = TopologicalSpace.Opens X` (a `CompleteLattice`), the categorical finite product coincides with `⨅` only up to the *propositional* (not `rfl`) equality `CategoryTheory.Limits.CompleteLattice.limit_eq_iInf` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/Lattice.lean:199`) — a `le_antisymm` proof, since `Opens X` is a thin/partial-order category (iso ⇒ equal by antisymmetry, but not definitionally).

**Confirmed empirically**: `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/Adelic/Cokernel.lean:346` proves exactly this comparison by hand:
```
theorem prodOpens_eq_iInf {m : ℕ} (j : Fin m → ι) :
    (∏ᶜ ((FormalCoproduct.mk _ 𝒰).obj ∘ j) : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) = ⨅ i, 𝒰 (j i) :=
  le_antisymm (le_iInf fun a => (Limits.Pi.π ((FormalCoproduct.mk _ 𝒰).obj ∘ j) a).le) ...
```
— a real `le_antisymm` lemma, i.e. genuinely not `rfl`. This is the only place in the project comparing the two Čech-term shapes, and it is done bespoke inside `Cokernel.lean` for a specific `ULift (Fin 2)` two-element cover (`AffineCoverMVSquare`), not as a reusable general lemma.

**Index-universe (`ULift`) note:** `cechComplexFunctor` requires `U : ι → C` with `ι : Type w` and `HasFiniteProducts C`; the project instantiates it with `ι : Type u` directly in `Scheme.cechCochain`. Elsewhere (`AffineSerreVanishing.lean`, e.g. `:238,:377,:493`) the flavour-(a) Spec-side lemmas systematically use `ι := ULift.{u} (Fin n)` to land finite index families in `Type u` (universe-pinning discipline for `Ideal.span`/`Finite ι` arguments living in `Type u`); `Cokernel.lean`'s `AffineCoverMVSquare.coverFamily` uses `ULift.{u} (Fin 2)` for the same reason feeding `cechComplexFunctor`.

**Conclusion for §2**: **No comparison lemma between the two flavours exists in the project** beyond the ad-hoc `prodOpens_eq_iInf`/`prodOpens_fin_one`/`prodOpens_fin_two` family in `Cokernel.lean` (grep confirms `cechComplexFunctor` appears only in `Cokernel.lean` and `Carriers.lean`; `sectionCechCosimplicial`/`sectionCechComplex` appear only in `CechBridge.lean`/`PresheafCech.lean`/`CechAcyclic.lean`/`CechToCohomology.lean`/`CechSectionIdentification*.lean` — **zero file imports/references both families**). Building the seed for `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf`/`Scheme.cechCohomology` (flavour b) will require either (i) reproving the algebraic-heart exactness directly against flavour-(b)'s term shape (degreewise `∏ᶜ` in `ModuleCat k`, via `Concrete.productEquiv`/`Pi.map`, mirroring `sectionCechProductEquiv` at `CechAcyclic.lean:1505`, but for the categorical `∏ᶜ` rather than `⨅`), or (ii) building a general term-by-term iso `Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰 ≅ (Ab-or-ModuleK-valued sectionCechComplex-analogue)` using `prodOpens_eq_iInf`-style lemmas degreewise, then transporting exactness/vanishing across that iso (§3).

---

## 3. Vanishing transport tools

Two already-built patterns, both in-project, plus one core Mathlib fact:

1. **Ab-flavour, same-category iso transport** — `AffineSerreVanishing.lean:424`:
```
theorem cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (e : F ≅ G) (p : ℕ)
    (h : IsZero (cechCohomology U F p)) : IsZero (cechCohomology U G p) :=
  h.of_iso ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor Ab.{u} (ComplexShape.up ℕ) p).mapIso
    ((sectionCechComplexFunctor U).mapIso e)).symm
```
This is the template: coefficient-presheaf iso ⇒ `sectionCechComplexFunctor`/`sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor` (`CechToCohomology.lean:85`/`:50`) mapIso ⇒ `HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor _ _ p` mapIso ⇒ `IsZero.of_iso`. The analogous route for `ModuleCat k`-valued complexes/`Scheme.cechCohomology` would need a `ModuleCat`-valued `cechComplexFunctor`-mapIso from a sheaf-of-`k`-modules iso (Mathlib's `cechComplexFunctor` is already functorial in the coefficient presheaf `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`, so `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).mapIso` is directly available — no project-local reconstruction needed here, unlike flavour (a)).

2. **`IsZero ↔ Subsingleton` bridge for `ModuleCat`** — Mathlib, `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Basic.lean:253,377,384`:
```
theorem ModuleCat.isZero_of_subsingleton (M : ModuleCat R) [Subsingleton M] : IsZero M
theorem ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (h : IsZero M) : Subsingleton M
ModuleCat.isZero_iff_subsingleton : IsZero M ↔ Subsingleton M
```
This is exactly the bridge needed since `Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover` (`Carriers.lean:612`) is phrased with `Subsingleton (Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n)` rather than `IsZero`. Project precedent: `FreePresheafComplex.lean:607` already uses `ModuleCat.isZero_of_subsingleton` in exactly this direction.

3. **Generic `IsZero.of_iso`** (`CategoryTheory.Limits.IsZero.of_iso`, standard Mathlib) transports `IsZero` across any iso in any category — this is what both patterns above bottom out in; no forget₂-specific machinery was needed (the homology objects being compared always live in the *same* target category `Ab`/`ModuleCat k`, so no forget₂-reflects-zero argument shows up anywhere in the project). If you *do* need to cross categories (e.g. compare an `Ab`-valued homology object with a `ModuleCat k`-valued one via `forget₂`), the project's only precedent for `forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat` faithfulness/iso-reflection is `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean:772` (`isLimitOfReflectsOfMapIsLimit (forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat) ...`) and `FreePresheafComplex.lean:408-410` (`Functor.map_isIso (forget₂ (ModuleCat _) Ab) _`) — i.e. `forget₂` reflects isos/limits (it's an equivalence onto its (additive, exact) essential image for a fixed ring), no bespoke "reflects zero objects" lemma exists but is derivable trivially from `forget₂`'s exactness (`isZero_of_subsingleton`/`subsingleton_of_isZero` transported along the forget₂'s underlying-type identification).

---

## 4. Basis/cofinality material and Mathlib generalisation lemmas for a general `X`

### `standard_cover_cofinal` (`AffineSerreVanishing.lean:167`)
```
theorem standard_cover_cofinal {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (f : R) {α : Type u}
    (W : α → (Spec R).Opens)
    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ ⨆ a, W a) :
    ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → α),
      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ∧
      ∀ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ W (φ i)
```
Spec-specific inputs used: `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen f`, `PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens`.

### `standard_cover_cofinal_affine` (`AffineSerreVanishing.lean:558`) — the D(f)→general-affine-open generalisation *already done* (Spec-side)
```
theorem standard_cover_cofinal_affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (V : (Spec R).Opens)
    (hV : IsAffineOpen V) {α : Type u} (W : α → (Spec R).Opens) (hcov : V ≤ ⨆ a, W a) :
    ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → α),
      V = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ∧
      ∀ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ W (φ i)
```
Only change from `standard_cover_cofinal`: `hK := hV.isCompact` (`IsAffineOpen.isCompact`) in place of `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen f`; same `isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens` + finite-subcover argument (identical proof shape, `V ⊓ W a` in place of `Uf ⊓ W a`). **This is the exact template to port to a general scheme `X`, affine open `U`**: the analogue needs `PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens` replaced by the basis-of-basic-opens-of-`U` statement `IsAffineOpen.isBasis_basicOpen` (used at `AffineScheme.lean:622`, `(isBasis_basicOpen U)`), and `PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen` replaced by `X.basicOpen (· : Γ(X,U))`.

### Mathlib v4.31 lemmas available for the port to a general `X`/affine `U` (all read in full from `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` and `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Scheme.lean`)

- **`Scheme.basicOpen`** (`Scheme.lean:655`, section `BasicOpen`, `variable (X : Scheme) {V U : X.Opens} (f g : Γ(X, U))`): `X.basicOpen (f : Γ(X,U)) : X.Opens`, keyed on sections over an **arbitrary** `U` (not just `⊤`) — exactly matches what's needed.
  - `Scheme.lean:687` `X.basicOpen_le : X.basicOpen f ≤ U` (tagged `@[sheaf_restrict]`).
  - `Scheme.lean:677` `X.basicOpen_res (i : op U ⟶ op V) : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map i f) = V ⊓ X.basicOpen f`.
  - `Scheme.lean:718` `X.basicOpen_mul : X.basicOpen (f * g) = X.basicOpen f ⊓ X.basicOpen g` — the "same-open sections" product formula (used exactly as `PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul` is used in `CechAcyclic.lean:1434`/`basicOpen_sprod` on the Spec side; ports verbatim to `sprod` for `X.basicOpen`).
  - `Scheme.lean:736` `X.basicOpen_one : X.basicOpen (1 : Γ(X,U)) = U`.
  - `Scheme.lean:739` `instance algebra_section_section_basicOpen (f : Γ(X,U)) : Algebra Γ(X,U) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) := (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (basicOpen_le f)).op).hom.toAlgebra` — **already a synthesizable instance** for the restriction algebra (note: `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen`'s docstring, `CechAcyclic.lean:2035`, claims "NOT a synthesizable instance" for the analogous `Algebra Γ(V) Γ(D a)` on `Spec R` — but the general-`X` `Scheme.basicOpen` API already provides it as an instance keyed on `X.basicOpen_le`; worth double-checking whether it fires automatically before reconstructing the ad-hoc `.toAlgebra` pattern).

- **`IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`** (`AffineScheme.lean:597`, `include hU`): `IsAffineOpen (X.basicOpen f)` for `hU : IsAffineOpen U`, `f : Γ(X,U)`.

- **`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen`** (`AffineScheme.lean:659`, `include hU`): `IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)` — the direct algebraic content: sections over `X.basicOpen f` are `Γ(X,U)_f`.

- **`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`** (`AffineScheme.lean:726`): `{V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) (e : V = X.basicOpen f) : @IsLocalization.Away _ _ f Γ(X,V) _ (X.presheaf.map i.op).hom.toAlgebra` — the exact lemma the Spec-side `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_affineOpen` (`CechAcyclic.lean:2073`) invokes for `hloc`.

- **`IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`** (`AffineScheme.lean:909`, `include hU`): `{s : Set Γ(X,U)} : ⨆ f : s, X.basicOpen (f : Γ(X,U)) = U ↔ Ideal.span s = ⊤` — **THE** "covering ⇔ spans unit ideal" criterion (name confirmed exactly). Companion: `IsAffineOpen.self_le_iSup_basicOpen_iff` (`:936`) is the `≤` form.

- **`IsAffineOpen.isCompact`** (`AffineScheme.lean:501`, `protected theorem`, `include hU`): `IsCompact (U : Set X)` — the quasi-compactness input `standard_cover_cofinal_affine` uses.

- **`IsAffineOpen.isBasis_basicOpen`** (used at `AffineScheme.lean:622`) — the "basic opens of `U` form a basis of `U`" fact, the general-`X` analogue of `PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens`, needed for the `exists_subset_of_mem_open` step of the cofinality proof.

- Instance `IsAffine.of_isIso`, `IsAffine X.basicOpen r` for `[IsAffine X]` (`AffineScheme.lean:608`, only for `⊤`-sections) vs. the general `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen` (works for any affine `U`, any `f : Γ(X,U)`) — use the latter for the curve case since `U` is a proper affine open, not all of `X`.

**Summary porting recipe for §4**: replace every `PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen`/`Spec R` occurrence in `standard_cover_cofinal_affine` with `X.basicOpen (· : Γ(X,U))`/`X`, `IsAffineOpen.isCompact` for quasi-compactness (already what `standard_cover_cofinal_affine` uses — no change needed there), and `IsAffineOpen.isBasis_basicOpen` in place of `PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens`. This is a mechanical, already-modeled port.

## 5. `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` — definition and restriction-map relation to `O_C`

Split across three files (`StructureSheafModuleK/{Presheaf,SheafProperty,Carriers}.lean`).

**`Presheaf.lean:171` — the presheaf, `toModuleKPresheaf`:**
```
noncomputable def Scheme.toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
    (Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
      map_add' := ...
      map_smul' := ... }   -- uses toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
```
So **as a type/underlying group, `toModuleKPresheaf.obj U` is literally `Γ(C.left, U) = O_C(U)`**; the restriction map is `O_C`'s own presheaf restriction map `(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom`, merely re-packaged as a `k`-linear map (`ModuleCat.ofHom` wraps the *same* function, `map_smul'` proved via naturality of the algebra map, `map_add'` trivial). There is no separate "sheaf of `k`-modules" data beyond `O_C` — the `k`-module structure comes from the algebra map below.

**`Presheaf.lean:126,134` — the `k`-algebra structure on each `Γ(C,U)`:**
```
noncomputable def kToSection (C) (U) : CommRingCat.of k ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫ C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
noncomputable instance algebraSection (C) (U) : Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
```
i.e. `algebraMap k Γ(C,U)` is the structure-morphism's global ring map `C.hom.app ⊤ : k → Γ(C,⊤)` restricted to `U`; `kToSection_naturality` (`:147`) and `algebraMap_naturality` (`:155`) show this is compatible with `O_C`'s own restriction maps, i.e. `toModuleKPresheaf`'s restriction maps *are* `O_C`'s restriction maps and they are `k`-algebra (hence `k`-linear) maps.

**`SheafProperty.lean:42` — the sheaf itself, `toModuleKSheaf`:**
```
noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
  ⟨toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C⟩
```
with `toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare` (`:52`, `Iso.refl _`!) confirming `forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat` applied to `toModuleKSheaf C` is **literally equal** (not just isomorphic) to the pre-existing `AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf `toAbSheaf C.left`.

**No existing lemma computes `toModuleKSheaf` sections on basic opens or relates to localization** (grep for `toModuleKPresheaf.*basicOpen`/`toModuleKSheaf.*[Ll]ocaliz` across the whole project returns nothing). This is a genuine gap: to run the algebraic heart (§1) against `toModuleKSheaf`, you will need to establish, for `f ∈ Γ(U, O_C)` (`U` affine open of `C.left`), that `(toModuleKSheaf C).presheaf.obj (op (C.left.basicOpen f))` is (as a `k`-module) the localisation `(Γ(U,O_C))_f`, via `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen` composed with the definitional identity `toModuleKPresheaf.obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)` (`Presheaf.lean:197`, `@[simp] toModuleKPresheaf_obj`, `rfl`) — i.e. the `k`-module carrier of the sheaf is definitionally `O_C`'s carrier, so `IsLocalization.Away` transports for free at the type level; only the `k`-linearity of the localisation-comparison maps (`AwayComparison.comparison`) needs re-establishing in the `ModuleCat k` bundling.
